There was also the magic crystal that was meant for the initial growth of the Reaper Mantis. Now, it had formed a volcanic eruption-like boosting force for her.

With this boost, it would be difficult for her growth to be slow.

Every day, the Reaper Mantis had a new breakthrough, and every day she had new improvements.

In the entire Beast Valley, no one besides the Sickle Weasel and Ryoma who tracked and observed them knew that the growth of this newborn beast had exceeded beyond compare.

Her growth rate, compared to the growth rate of the other Battle-type Beasts cultivated by other challengers, were a hundred times higher.

Perhaps even hundreds of times.

s

Even if there were countless great people who came to the Beast Valley to challenge the Level, Yue Yang's speed of cultivating the Reaper Mantis was unprecedented. Yue Yang had found out about the two followers, the Sickle Weasel and Ryoma, a long time ago but just ignored them. Sometimes, keeping one or two pieces will be the key to a good game of chess. Student Yue Yang not only thought so, but he also taught the Reaper Mantis this concept.

"Okay!" The Reaper Mantis nodded obediently.

This time, she didn't fully understand.

However, she had already engraved Yue Yang's words firmly in her heart.

The teachings that she didn't understand now will surely be understood when she has a higher level of wisdom in the future.

chapters.title: Chapter 859 I Just Want To Be Free!
chapters.body:
Chapter 859 I Just Want To Be Free!

It was now the 15th day since they entered the Beast Valley.

The Reaper Mantis who was cultivating under the waterfall, flashed a burst of colorful brilliance.

The beam of energy that was rising above her head made an impact with the waterfall, making it go backwards. The water splashes were turned upside down and shot back toward the sky, forming an incredible sight.

The shockwave spreaded towards the surroundings.

This energy circle spreaded extremely fast, but surprisingly, the surrounding flowers and trees were not hurt, whereas the grazing deer, sheep, cattle, and horses were not surprised either. They just raised their heads in unison, looking at the energy beam that rose to the sky with awe-inspiring eyes. Sickle Weasel and Ryoma, who were resting under the shade of the trees, awakened from their sleep and leaped to the top of the tree together, looking at the golden energy beam on the other side of the hill pointing to the sky. The golden beam of light even exceeded the height of the peak of the hill that had a cascading waterfall.

Although they were separated by more than ten kilometers, the energy fluctuations and intimidating will greatly shocked the hearts of Sickle Weasel and Ryoma.

After a long time, they saw the golden beam of light disappear.

The sickle swallowed a mouthful of saliva with difficulty, "In ten days, the newborn beast grew into a Quasi-Holy Beast; With just only 5 days later, she was promoted to a Holy Beast t! The five Beast Kings will never be able to reach the realm of the Holy Beast even if they trained for their entire lifetime. Yet, she managed to do so just by using half a month's time. W-what kind of potential was this?"

Ryoma's voice trembled a little before he guessed, "Maybe she was originally a Holy Beast!"

"Impossible, no one would take the risk of using a Holy Beast. Letting a Holy Beast walk into the Gates of Reincarnation? What if it was eliminated at its initial stage…?" Sickle Weasel waved its hand again and again.

s

"He has Pandora's Box, which is protected by the Divine Power of Calamity; there's nothing he could lose. Plus, a person like him wouldn't just own a Holy Beast. I actually think that he might still have a Divine Beast. Otherwise, it would be impossible for him to be this familiar with cultivating Holy Beasts!"

The Sickle Weasel became silent after hearing Ryoma's words.

It seemed like that was really the case!

That kid was not as simplistic as merely cultivating a Holy Beast to pass the Level. If his goal was just wanting to clear the Beast Valley, he could have left five days ago. With the wisdom of that newborn beast and the various trials that remain up ahead, it wouldn't pose a problem for that Holy Beast. He wasn't satisfied with the Quasi-Holy Tier, so he trained her to the point where she became a Holy Beast. With just half a month into his plans, he wouldn't ever dream of stopping the progress.

The tier of the Divine Beast was the ultimate goal of the newcomer.

In the Beast Valley, was any challenger able to cultivate a Divine Beast?

The Sickle Weasel looked at Ryoma with a puzzled expression, but Ryoma kept shaking his head. He had been trapped in the Beast Valley for thousands of years, and within that time, he had never heard of anyone who could cultivate a Divine Beast in the Beast Valley!

If this newcomer could really cultivate a Divine Beast, what will happen to the Beast Valley?

All the residents and failed challengers would probably turn into Demonic Beast, right? Adding everyone's energies together, how could it not be sufficient to nurture a Divine Beast?

"Hmm?"

Ryoma motioned for the Sickle Weasel to hold its breath.

This was to watch out for the vulture that often circulates in the sky to spy as an intelligence report. Although that guy was scouting information on behalf of the Lion King, it never dared to get too close, mostly soaring across the sky. As long as its target on the ground was still there, it would return immediately. The Sickle Weasel slid into the canopy of the tree, not only blending with nature, but also wearing a cloak of invisibility outside its body.

An afterimage crossed the sky as fast as a meteor.

With the eyesight of Ryoma and the Sickle Weasel, it was actually very difficult to capture this shadow.

However, Ryoma found that the huge target in the shadow's hands…was a stiff vulture.

Human head, butterfly wings on the frame of a bee— this was the ‘Poisonous Bee King’, one of the five Kings of the Beast Regiments.

But why did the Poisonous Bee capture the vulture alive?

Ignoring the fact that it had captured the vulture alive, the Sickle Weasel and Ryoma were more confused about why it brought the vulture as it flew towards the waterfall near the hill. Wasn't it afraid of the curse of the Divine Power of Calamity?

"Today is really a good day, there are guests here again," By the side of the waterfall, Yue Yang comfortably washed his feet with cool water while lying lazily on the rocks. He basked in the sun as he relished in the Reaper Mantis's light massage on his shoulders. Occasionally, he also opened his mouth to eat a crystal grape that the Reaper Mantis had fed into his mouth.

"There are many kinds of guests," The Poisonous Bee King threw the stiff vulture on the ground.

"Then which one do you belong to?" Yue Yang asked with a smile.

"I'm different from the guests who met their deaths and were made into nutrients to upgrade your newborn beast. I belong to the one that can be trusted, the one that proposes a cooperation," The Poisonous Bee King landed on the rock beside Yue Yang and laid a finger on the stunned but very much alive vulture, chuckling softly, "Consider this my meeting gift for you.. If you are willing to believe me, I will show you more of my sincerity. You can trust me— before your beast evolves into a Holy Beast, I will do my utmost best to fulfill that wish of yours!"

"Okay, but can you tell me the reason?" Yue Yang chuckled and said, "You probably aren't going to tell me that you're a good Samaritan, born to enjoy doing good deeds for others, are you?"

"I only need one thing," The Poisonous Bee King's expression suddenly became serious.

"And what is that?" Yue Yang asked rhetorically.

At this time, Ryoma and the Sickle Weasel had closed the distance to a mere three kilometers with their ears sharpened.

They want to know what kind of deal would the Poisonous Bee King make with this young man. Was it to assassinate the Lion King and seize the position of being on top among the five Beast Kings? Or was it to defeat the double-headed Black Dragon that was guarding the Wisdom Fruit before it itself would take the fruit to enhance its wisdom?

No matter what the conditions are, they believe that it will be a huge deal that will shake the balance of the Beast Valley, bringing change to the whole situation!

The Poisonous Bee King groaned slightly.

In the end, it uttered a word that made the Sickle Weasel and Ryoma almost scream, "I want to be free! I don't want anything else, I just want to be free!"

Freedom!

This was something that the Heaven Realm never lacked, as even those ant-like subsidiary races below the normal level have their fair share of freedom. Perhaps the freedom of those people were cut short and was not considered true freedom, but even that discounted freedom was a luxury that all the lives in the Beast Valley couldn't obtain!

The Beast Valley had everything; nothing was scarce.

Except for the fact that there was no freedom!

Ryoma and the Sickle Weasel had the opinion that they had forgotten this word, but now they can't help but burst into tears when they heard it.

When one has freedom, they will never know of its preciousness; but when that same person loses their freedom, only then will they realise its meaning!

Freedom… This is something that anyone can have, but once lost, even the whole world cannot be exchanged for it. It is precious, something that no treasure in the world could exchange with, something that couldn't be replaced. Only those who have lost their freedom will understand that one can have nothing— no money, house, a comfortable life, the wealth of the enemy country or a famous name. Sometimes, there may even be none of the romantic relationships that everyone desires, but nothing would be as uniquely important as freedom!

People who have freedom will never understand the pain of losing it.

Those who lost their freedom were outcasts of fate.

It is the elimination of time, the silt of the long river of history and the most painfully desperate existence in the world. That type of torment could only be described as a dark abyss!

Yue Yang smiled, "Do you think I will agree to this? Even if I do agree, do you think I can give you freedom? This is the Beast Valley!"

The Poisonous Bee covered its face with its hands and sobbed very softly, "You, the proud son of Heaven, will never understand how hard I have been struggling these years. For six thousand years, I have tried to survive. From a kind-hearted woman who never kills, I have turned into a Poisonous Queen Bee in Beast Valley. The number of people I have killed over the years have been several times greater than the people I knew in the first half of my life. But, even in my most desperate times, the thirst for freedom has never left in my heart for even a second. It is the light that illuminated my difficult path of advancement… In order for me to be free, my father and husband both collapsed in front of me. They used their lives to open a bloody path for me, where at the same time, they pinned their hopes on me. In order for me to move forward smoothly towards the path of freedom, my mother sacrificed her chastity for decades. It wasn't until I grew up and had the power to protect myself that she could bear to kill herself in front of my eyes."

T/L Note: Once again, the bee has her gender clarified, so yes, she'll be referred to as a ‘her' from here on.

Yue Yang still shook his head, "I sympathize with your tragedy, but the Beast Valley has its own Laws. I'm sorry, I can't help you."

The Poisonous Queen Bee quickly wiped away her tears and waved her hand, "About this, I know of a secret. About six thousand years ago, when I was not fully grown and I was very wary of everything around me, there was a God-like man who came. He told me of a condition, that as long as there was a contract under a Holy Beast, where I would become a beast used by the Holy Beast, I would be able to leave. But back then I was still a resident and I was still skeptical of the God-like man, so I rejected his kindness. At that time, I was a resident who had two chances, where I had to commit suicide and become a Beast to get a contract with him. I missed that opportunity at the time…Who knew that I had to wait for six thousand years for my second chance to arrive!"

Yue Yang asked curiously, "Then why do you dare to trust me? Do you think I am trustworthy?"

The Poisonous Queen Bee paused slightly, "If we were enemies, it would indeed be a very terrible thing. You are not a kind-hearted gentleman. On the contrary, your boldly decisive attitude and your concept of treating enemies like ants will make others shiver and tremble in their hearts. However, if I can become your friend, then I believe it would be a very lucky thing. For freedom, I am willing to take risks and believe in you once. After all, you are the most compatible one I have encountered in six thousand years."

"There is another reason why I am willing to trust you," The Poisonous Queen Bee stared into Yue Yang's eyes, "That day, that God-like man, his aura was very similar to yours… Maybe you are not relatives but you must be of a similar kind, coming from the same place."

"What's the name of the person you are talking about?" Yue Yang asked.

"That God-like man is called Zhan Feng. I have heard his enemies call him the ‘Prison Emperor’!" The Poisonous Queen Bee will never forget this name and the silhouette of the man who sighed when he had walked away.

"It's him again," Student Yue Yang was a little unhappy. How was it possible that so many people actually knew who he was? It's been six thousand years, yet, his name couldn't be forgotten and was rather long-lasting. However, since he had some relations with the Prison Emperor, Yue Yang was too embarrassed to refuse. How was he supposed to say, I used the Prison Emperor's Divine Seal to crack open chestnuts. Also, the Prison Emperor's Divine Sword in Tigress's hands and the Prison Emperor's Divine Sceptre of Wu Xia's possession, were all left behind by him.

In the face of equipment and treasures that he basically obtained for free, it didn't sit right with him if he didn't show a bit of respect to his name.

Forget it.

He just thought of it as doing good deeds to accumulate character points!

Student Yue Yang asked the last question, "Your parents and husband have died here, and you have been trapped here for six thousand years. I don't think that you would have any other relatives out there by now, so was there still any point in going out?"

A longing sliver of light flashed in the eyes of the Poisonous Queen Bee, "I promised them that I would take their ashes back home and bury them on the hilltops of my hometown. I also promised my mother that I would hand over important tokens of the family to my younger sister. From my hands, I would pass the throne to her, let her lead the Bee Clan to glory. If my sister was gone, then I would pass it on to her descendants. I not only have lived with the hopes of my relatives, but also, in my own heart, I have longed for a day where I can leave. Even if it’s just one day of freedom, just let me look at the liberating sky in the outside world and the home I used to live in. Then, I can die with no regrets…"

As long as she had one day of freedom, she was able to die without regrets?

These words shook the heart of Yue Yang's Supreme Will.

He may not be able to help with other things, but this kind of problem that could be agreed with a nod of his head, was still worth considering for the sake of the hopes and beliefs she had for six thousand years.

"Huuuuuu!" The Reaper Mantis was touched by the words of the Queen Bee to the point where she burst into a fit of tears.

s

"…" Ryoma and the Sickle Weasel also started to shed tears of their own.

If it weren't for the fear of revealing their act of pursuit as they grit their teeth, they would have liked to cry out loud.

Freedom, this was the freedom that they dared not dream of! If they could regain their freedom, as long as they could live happily for one day under the skies of freedom, they could really die without regrets.

In the blurry eyes of tears, the two understood the twinkling light that reflected in each other's eyes:

I want to be free as well!

chapters.title: Chapter 860 Cultivation And The First Test
chapters.body:
Chapter 860 Cultivation And The First Test

"…" The Poisonous Queen Bee suddenly used a secret language.

"…" Yue Yang even used some Heavenly Runes as an aid to the silent conversation.

Both Ryoma and the Sickle Weasel, who were watching from a distance, were dumbfounded. Needless to say, the secret language was definitely inaudible, but the two of them couldn't figure out the meaning of the Heavenly Rune patterns that were clearly visible. In fact, Ryoma and the Sickle Weasel were not completely ignorant of the Heavenly Runes. They recognized a lot of the Heavenly Runes, but after forming them into different patterns, all they could do was stare.

What were the two talking about?

It must be a matter of cooperation.

The question is, how will the Poisonous Queen Bee and this young man cooperate, and what plans will they have between them?

They talked for about ten minutes.

After talking to Yue Yang about their further cooperation, the Poisonous Queen Bee spread her wings and flew away from the waterfall happily.

Intentionally or unconsciously, she glanced at the on the side of the forest where Ryoma and the Sickle Weasel hid, which made the duo's heartbeats almost stop in fright.

The strength of the Poisonous Queen Bee is as high as the 6th Level of the Heaven Rank. This meant that both the Sickle Weasel and Ryoma were incomparable to her level.

s

Fortunately, the Poisonous Queen Bee only glanced at them randomly and flew away at a swift speed.

The Reaper Mantis killed the unluckily stiff vulture. It was an energy supplement from the Queen Bee anyways, so there was no reason to waste it. In order to become stronger, the Reaper Mantis had practiced very hard, hoping to reach the standards of her owner's mind earlier. Of course, she also understood how far a gap in power there was between Divine Beasts and Holy Beasts—it was almost like the gap of crossing an insurmountable moat. In addition to working hard, she also needed to be lucky, just like the Golden Crown Thorny Flower Queen Duo Duo, Jiang Ying, Red and Ah Man. They were all talented existences, but they have also paid an unknown amount of blood and sweat to obtain the achievements they had today.

She still had a long way to go before she could catch up with Duo Duo, Red and the others.

Not to mention Xiao Wen Li, who was born into the Diamond Rank.

"Let's proceed according to the plan!" Yue Yang asked the Reaper Mantis to postpone her constant practice and took her for a day off, where they walked through the waterfall to the middle section of the Beast Valley.

Ryoma and the Sickle Weasel looked at each other.

Should we continue to keep up, or should we just let them go?

If they stopped here, their lives could be guaranteed. If there were no accidents, they could practically stay alive in the Beast Valley forever.

But what the two wanted now was not safety. Instead, they wanted something that they could only dream of, that is ‘freedom'. With this young man, Ryoma and the Sickle Weasel weren't sure if they could be free. After all, they were not the Poisonous Queen Bee, let alone have any relations with the man who vowed to help her long ago. By following him, they might end up becoming a tonic just like that vulture.

But if they don't keep up, their freedom will slip from their grasps forever.

For the ‘freedom' that they could only long for in their hearts, the two gritted their teeth and nodded to each other again, eager to fight and strive for a foolish sliver of hope!

The Beast Valley.

Of course, it was not an actual valley, but a huge and independent world.

Even if the sizes of the Rain Valley, Wind Valley, Sand Valley and Desire valley were combined, the Beast Valley remains unprecedentedly larger. Strictly speaking, it could be divided into five major regions.

Except for the three large areas with relatively harsh environments where only a few people were involved, there were two kinds of dominant forces fighting against each other in another two areas. They were the residents, led by the Three Chiefs, and the Battle-type Beasts led by the five Beast Kings. The Lion King, the Eagle King, the Human-Faced Tiger, the Toad King and the Poisonous Bee Queen were the five Beast Kings, each of which governing a varying number of Battle-type Beasts, gathering them together for the common good with a lax management method.

Among them, the Lion King is the strongest, whereas the Eagle King is the weakest.

Because the advantages of each Beast King are different, the Kings that were chosen to rely on by the failed challengers who turned into beasts, differed as well based on preferences.

The more ferocious and carnivorous beasts mostly rely on the Lion King, while the fowl beasts that were good at flying were more willing to be under the reign of the Eagle King. Of course, there are many examples like the vulture who voluntarily served the Lion King as well.

According to various categories, the beasts were mostly casted towards the Lion King, the birds prefered the Eagle King, the reptiles were mostly under the Toad King, the aquatic species were the subordinates of the Human-Faced Tiger, and the insects beasts were the symbol of the Poisonous Queen Bee. In addition to the five major forces, the Beast Valley also had some stragglers wandering outside. If they were not strong enough or if they had the confidence to survive alone, they would not be recognized by the five major forces for the time being. Adding up the five forces, the number of beasts have already exceeded 3000, but those who were under the direct command of the Beast Kings were actually less than half. After all, most of the beasts just used the names of the Kings.

The number of residents was less than one thousand.

They were governed by the Three Chiefs.

But their strength was generally stronger than that of beasts. So as a final say, the residents must surpass the beasts.

The living conditions were the easiest in the initial area of the Beast Valley, mainly the Beast Valley Fortress and some active sites for skirmishers. At the same time, the various materials and cultivation environment here were far inferior to the middle area of the Beast Valley. As for the rear, the Blood Flower Forest where the Beast Valley's Level clearance portal was located, it was an area of death that no one dared to take lightly as the living conditions were extremely harsh.

There were many residents who knew that there were ‘Wisdom Fruits’ to be picked in the Blood Flower Forest. If obtained in a short time, it is very likely that their beasts will evolve their wisdom and successfully clear the Level to leave, but they dared not try it out.

Because there was a Double-Headed Black Dragon guarding the Fruits of Wisdom.

If one didn't have enough strength and attempted to pick the fruit, you'd only be served as a course dinner for the Double-Headed Black Dragon, changing the ‘menu' from its usual dinner.

"Huh?"

The Sickle Weasel and Ryoma followed them the entire way.

Trailing behind Yue Yang's figure from a distance, they stepped into the middle area of the valley that they rarely entered.

At dusk, Yue Yang seemed to be walking forward without being aware of the danger around him. A wild and poisonous python that had been waiting for an opportunity laid in ambush behind the jungle. It suddenly attacked, intending to swallow the Reaper Mantis in one bite as it opened its huge mouth and tried to bite down viciously. The Sickle Weasel and Ryoma both saw it and they wanted to remind them, but they were afraid that doing so would do little to help with the battle. On the contrary, they would expose their presence.

Whoosh!

The Reaper Mantis was waving her arm that was gradually evolving into a sickle.

In the sky, two diagonally crossed rose patterns were drawn.

The wildly poisonous python stagnated, and its long neck seemed to be as if it was frozen in time as the two rose patterns swished by.

In an instant, its body and the head separated.

It was a clean cut at the broken neck, where a burst of crimson black blood was sprayed frantically.

The 2nd Level Heaven Ranked python was instantly killed by the Reaper Mantis on the spot!

The huge body crashed to the ground.

"Scarlet Rose, my God, she really changed my Cross Lightning Kill to the shape of a diagonally crossing rose. Here, these two ‘Scarlet Roses' that split the sky and instantly killed the enemy are way too beautiful!" The Sickle Weasel couldn't help but shower her in praise. On the basis of being the original creator of the Cross Lightning Kill, she had developed it into the level of an Instantaneous Kill. The Sickle Weasel thought that it had already reached the limit of its fighting skills. Unexpectedly after only five days, this little thing, under the guidance of that young man, had reached the realm of the Cross Scarlet Rose.

This made it, who had studied the skills for thousands of years, really wanted to pound its own head on the ground to just meet its end.

There is always going to be a gap between people.

But it didn’t have to be that big, right?

Yue Yang shook his head with slight dissatisfaction. Although the Scarlet Rose attack was successful, it was still too far away from the goal he asked for, which was ‘each petal should be able to split nine spaces and the ten-character picture array as a whole'. It's just that today was a holiday for her and the shot just now was just a small hunt outside of training, so he didn't criticize it strictly.

He stretched out his big hand and stroked the small head of the reincarnated Reaper Mantis.

After the Reaper Mantis turned into a Holy Beast, her body was completely transformed into a human being.

Insect limbs no longer exist.

Her entire being looked like a small, exquisite, cute, and very miniscule-sized human. The only difference from ordinary humans would be her wings. They have also remained, and the golden wings have evolved into colorful wings that resemble the hues of rainbows.

At first, she thought that her master would severely criticize her. Who would have thought that he just stroked her little head lightly.

At this time, she was very touched.

She couldn't help but spit out her little pink tongue mischievously, flying up to Yue Yang's shoulder happily and her little fist lightly pounded his shoulder. She had learned a lot from the sweet-talking Hui Tai Lang. Everyone knew, apart from Hui Tai Lang, no one else liked to please Yue Yang like her.

The Reaper Mantis was encouraged by Yue Yang to hunt and kill some beasts all the way, training his fighting skills.

Besides the Scarlet Rose.

There was also the ‘Deer-Cutting Slash', modified by Yue Yang after stealing it from Bai Lu, the ‘Insane Eighteen Slashes' which was altered from the Sickle Weasel's Madness Sickle Tornado, the ‘Sonic Boom' and the ‘Reverse Finger Whirling' of the Scarlet-Eyed Beast that were stolen and modified. The ‘Large Walking Explosion' was also taken from the Toad King’s Ice Sword Storm. The fighting skills she learned secretly, after improvement and comprehension, were completely out of the blue and absolutely lethal. But some of the attributes were opposites, not necessarily the same as those cast by the original creator.

Ryoma and the Sickle Weasel also noticed one thing.

That cute little thing had been learning a mysterious technique, which seems to have been taught by the young man.

But she had never used it before, and they didn't know whether she was deliberately storing the skill or did she not meet the basic standard for casting it.

Three days later.

The wind was calm, and it seemed that everyone had forgotten Yue Yang.

Even the Poisonous Queen Bee, who had silently approached him before and boldly talked about cooperation with Yue Yang, was nowhere to be seen at this time.

Both Ryoma and the Sickle Weasel felt a little odd. This was not like a ‘safe and sound' appearance, but slightly more similar to the ‘calm before the storm'. Should they start to evacuate, so as to not involve themselves in troubling matters? After another ideological struggle, Ryoma and the Sickle Weasel finally clenched their teeth and persevered.

Freedom, like the dawn, tempted them and made them unwilling to turn around to leave.

And there were two people, so they didn't really feel lonely in their hearts.

Although they were not close friends, it seemed like they could comfort each other in their hearts and share their fears as they stood on the same front.

If they were both alone..

s

They most definitely couldn't stick to it anymore.

Yue Yang came to the middle section of the Beast Valley. He took the Reaper Mantis and went to the place where the first test of the Beast Valley was truly held— the Spirit Beast Great Hall. This was the first level to test the challengers' beasts, not knowing just how many beasts have been eliminated here. The first test was actually very simple, just requiring the challengers to do three things. This was known to all of the challengers and their Battle-type Beasts, but it just couldn't be done, being so close yet so far.

The first thing the Battle-type Beast has to do is to enter the Spirit Beast Great Hall of the first level and take out the sign that represents the identity of the beast.

This must be done by the Battle-type Beast alone.

No matter how powerful the owner is, he or she cannot provide any help. This criteria was also the most difficult part of the test.

chapters.title: Chapter 861 Advancing Alone, The Spirit Beast Great Hall
chapters.body:
Chapter 861 Advancing Alone, The Spirit Beast Great Hall

"Welcome, welcome!" When Yue Yang brought the Reaper Mantis to the test hall of the first level, the Toad King was waiting in front of the Great Hall. It slapped its belly and made a laugh that was revolting to hear, compelling others to vomit at the sound. It was no exaggeration to say that others' farts were a hundred times better than its laughter.

"It's you, Toad? What do you want to do?" Yue Yang knew that there must be nothing good about this guy being here.

"I'm being kind, just here to remind you that if you fail the first test, the beast will be punished by the Law and die. The first test is where most challengers fail, young man. Good luck! "The Toad King's ugly lips kept opening and closing, constantly bursting with laughter. It didn't wait for Yue Yang to speak and immediately turned and left, as if it was afraid of Yue Yang's curse. Before leaving however, it turned its head back and kindly reminded him, "Although the Beast Valley's Law will protect the challenger, the Box Hall is not a safe area for the beasts. Consider this my free gift of information for you! If there is something in the Box Hall that attacks when your beast pushes the box, don't say that I didn't remind you, hahaha!"

The Toad King gave one last laugh with a very unpleasant bad breath before it left.

Yue Yang pondered slightly.

Finally, he signaled the Reaper Mantis to enter the hall and take part in the test. Ryoma and the Sickle Weasel, who were hiding in the distance, became anxious. Should they tell him the truth about the Great Hall? If they showed up now and told him the truth about the Great Hall, it would surely be found out by the Toad King and the others; but if they don't go out and tell him, where it results in the little one failing the test.

Ryoma and the Sickle Weasel were in a dilemma.

If this young man fails, how could they talk about freedom?

They hoped that he wouldn't be annihilated just like that, as it wasn't easy to wait for the arrival of such a person either!

Ryoma motioned for the Sickle Weasel to not to show itself. After all, as a resident, he still had another chance even if his own beast died. He decided to stand up and tell Yue Yang about the trap in the Spirit Beast Great Hall.

s

Even if he would be attacked by the Toad King and the others afterwards, he couldn't care less about it.

The Sickle Weasel also gritted its teeth.

It looked towards the direction of where the Toad King had disappeared into. If Ryoma was attacked, it would rush out without hesitation to stop the blow and help him escape.

It was impossible to change the past, but today, the Sickle Weasel and Ryoma were on the same page. Both of them must fight for the hope in their hearts and for their coveted freedom!

"What are you doing here?"

A beautiful and melodious voice rang from behind Ryoma and the Sickle Weasel.

Although the sound was as sweet as the sound of nature, the two only felt a wave of coldness spread all over their frames, as if there was a chilling energy sliding down the back of their skulls along their spines. It was the Poisonous Queen Bee, whose hands were gently pressing onto the backs of both Ryoma and the Sickle Weasel. With her strength, as long as she wanted, she could make the two of their bodies freeze, leaving them with nothing but a step away from death— a ‘Living Corpse'. The Poisonous Queen Bee's stinger tail was an ultimate attack in the Beast Valley; as long as one was hit, it would definitely die!

Ryoma and the Sickle Weasel were both stunned.

For a moment, they were unsure of what to do.

The Poisonous Queen Bee suddenly retracted her hands and snorted, "Fellow friends who hide your heads but reveal your tails, you probably think you two were very well concealed with your Cloaking Skills. In fact, you are not even close— even the blind could see through it at a glance! However, this Queen is in a good mood today. As long as you two don't meddle in things that are not your business, I can temporarily leave your head attached on your neck. Remember, talk less and observe more, don't do anything else. You don't have to speak up here, and there's also no reason for you to speak up, okay?"

There seemed to be a shadow flashing in the distance, and the Poisonous Queen Bee immediately stopped.

She held her breath, as if doing so would make her invisible.

Standing behind Ryoma and the Sickle Weasel, it seemed like she did not exist as her concealment skills were many times stronger than Ryoma.

The Poisonous Queen Bee moved silently and disappeared at an extremely fast speed, as if nothing had ever appeared before.

Ryoma and the Sickle Weasel broke out in cold sweat.

This battlefield will be a conversation between the strong. With their strength, maybe apart from watching, there was really no way for them to change anything. There was a tall shadow coming from a distance which was at a Cyclops [1] that was at the height of six meters.

T/L Note: [1] A Cyclops is a one-eyed giant. The eye is usually in the middle of its head, and it's more like a human, just enlarged.

The Cyclops possessed the strength of the 4th Level Heaven Rank.

Although it was not at a very high level, judging from the gleaming light in its eyes, everyone knew that its wisdom was not low. At the very least, it was far smarter and more cunning than the ordinary cyclops. Without even looking at Yue Yang, the Cyclops rushed directly into the Spirit Beast Great Hall.

"Huh?" Yue Yang felt a little strange.

Did the test of Beast Valley require the challengers to line up? To the extent of someone rushing into it?

Ryoma and the Sickle Weasel knew what was going on. In the Spirit Beast Great Hall, there were hundreds of boxes of various textures distributed in the upper, middle, and lower spaces. Among them, there was a 5-Element Box that was the most precious. As long as one opened it, they could get a sign that represented the identity of the beast. The more advanced 5-Element Box that they opened, the more advanced a sign that they would get.

Of course, the more advanced the 5-Element Box was, the harder it was to find and open as well.

The best box required the aid of a companion. Only then could challengers open up specific locks.

For example, there are some Space Doors that will automatically close when someone enters. And if a single beast enters, it will no longer be able to get out for its entire lifetime. So, a reliable companion was needed to open the door outside. In the same way, there was another kind of Space Door that would not open again as long as someone entered. There was even a specific kind of Space Door that would not operate if someone was standing on top of certain structures. It could be said that the Beast Spirit Great Hall was a testing area that is both very easy to cause harm to enemies and requires the support of companions.

Unless it's just a common sign that was the easiest to obtain on the first floor of the Spirit Beast Great Hall.

In that case, the reward would be the least.

And it would be easy to be punished by law. On the first floor of the Spirit Beast Great Hall, most of the boxes are wooden boxes. Some of these wooden boxes needed to be placed in a certain shape to successfully open the door. If they were placed incorrectly or if a wooden box was broken, the consequences would be very serious. There would be a 50% chance that the Law would randomly usher in ten wild beasts from the Beast Valley, and there was also a 30% chance that thunder would emerge from the sky.

There is only a mere 20% chance that the beast would be safe and free from punishment.

Ryoma and the Sickle Weasel all knew that without even guessing— the Cyclops must be trying to make an ambush on the first floor, deliberately inducing any opponents to break the box.

If this does not kill the enemy, then it will rush to the second floor and stand on some structures to block the way.

To the point where it would even advance to the third floor and scurry beyond the Space Doors.

This would prevent the other side from entering, letting the other party be forced to choose a common sign. These kinds of tricks have long been played in the Beast Valley. Everyone knew how to sabotage another, but no one knew how to solve their own problems.

"Maybe there is more than just one Cyclops. It might just be the target that the Toad King deliberately wanted to expose," The Cyclops was not the one that Ryoma was concerned about the most.

"Maybe, she might have a way to solve it!" The Sickle Weasel said comfortingly.

That little one has an impressive amount of wisdom.

How could an identity test of the Spirit Beast Great Hall stump her? Even if a Cyclops caused trouble, she must be able to pass the test calmly. After all, she could reach the existence of a Holy Beast in ten and a half days! It was not an ordinary beast at all. Besides, this was the easiest test of the first level, which is also the test that had the highest success rate in the Beast Valley!

Yue Yang sat calmly on the steps of the Spirit Beast Great Hall. He took out a summer mat and fell asleep comfortably.

He did not seem like the master of a Battle-type Beast who was going to take part in the test. Instead, he resembled the look of a lazy test examiner.

The Reaper Mantis tilted her head to think.

After a long time, she slowly flew into the Spirit Beast Great Hall.

No one told her what to do, and no one could help her either. In the Great Hall of the Spirit Beasts, everything relied solely on her own efforts!

The huge shadow of theToad King flickered in a very distant direction. It was confirming the actions of the Reaper Mantis, confirming that she had entered the Spirit Beast Great Hall, before it left immediately. Based on the experience of Ryoma and the Sickle Weasel, it was discovered that the ugly Toad King hadn't really left. Instead, it just lurked to the second test of the first level, where this was indeed the case. The Toad King had taken completely destructive measures and placed interceptions one after another, each becoming more and more deadly. This had to be done in order to kill this Holy Beast with infinite potential, in order to wash away its own shame, and in order to re-establish itself as a Beast King. The prestige of the Toad King had already been decided, and he will not give up until he kills the little thing called the Reaper Mantis.

In the Spirit Beast Great Hall.

In addition to the Cyclops who rushed in, there was another shadow poking its head around.

Seeing that Yue Yang was asleep, it also slipped in silently. The Reaper Mantis didn't pay attention to any odd Cyclops; she just kept a cautious awareness and sneaked in quietly.

Inside, the space was surprisingly large. Besides the necessary passages and corridors, it was mostly divided into rooms. These rooms were similarly designed, but they came with different door numbers. In each room, there were boxes of different sizes as well.

When the Reaper Mantis tried to move a big box out with her mind, to her surprise, the door of the other room closed.

And when she put back the large box, the other door opened again.

These should be weight-balanced houses.

The Reaper Mantis arrived at this answer. When she walked to a mechanism bridge, she immediately understood why there were boxes in the room. These boxes were used for weight gain. When she put a big box on the bridge head mechanism, the deck that looked like a broken bridge immediately stretched by a meter. The Reaper Mantis added a stone to the pile, which was ruthlessly electrocuted by a strike of thunder by the Law. It seemed that it would be impossible to get past this mechanism bridge besides bringing in enough boxes.

If she didn't take the mechanism bridge, she could also take another route.

A route with many corridors in it.

Among them, at the end of a corridor, the Cyclops peeked out of the door and glanced at the Reaper Mantis.

Reaper Mantis remembered the master’s teaching in her mind. In any situation, she must be calm and be vigilant. Look around, use her wisdom to formulate strategies, combining brains and brawn, all to defeat the enemy. Compared to defeating the enemy with brute force, this method would be a hundred times easier… Thinking of this, her eyes became firm, and the sunny smile on Yue Yang's usual expression also appeared on her small face.

Any enemy who had seen this smile would feel terrified in their hearts.

s

Because this kind of smile was actually a hundred times more terrifying than the Devil's smile!

"Come on!" The Reaper Mantis clenched her small fist and gave herself a boost. Then, she floated towards the long-routed corridor where the Cyclops disappeared into.

With her master’s instruction, were there any difficulties in this world that could not be faced?

I must work hard!

I have to work harder than Red, Ah Man and Duo Duo. I must catch up with them as soon as possible and I must not let the master down. I've got to work hard, work hard… With a strong will to fight, the Reaper Mantis started to take action.

chapters.title: Chapter 862 Scheming Tricks? Fighting Fire With Fire
chapters.body:
Chapter 862 Scheming Tricks? Fighting Fire With Fire

Chapter 862: Scheming Tricks? Fighting Fire With Fire

Turning around the hallway, the situation here was not the same as when she came in.

In the spacious area, there were obvious visible traps, frozen pools sealed by Cold Energy, and flame nozzles that were in the shape of beasts’ mouths. According to this kind of terrain, they must be avoided so as not to trigger the mechanism.

The consequences would be disastrous if one triggers the mechanism.

Ordinarily foolish beasts wouldn’t be tricked, let alone the Reaper Mantis.

Only blinded idiots would fall for this kind of obvious trick.

The most conspicuous flaw was that there were shallow footprints on the ground at every certain distance.

That was where the Cyclops peeked out from.

These footprints have rather visible concealment attempts, most of them were signs that the Cyclops had tiptoed its way through. It was so tall but its footprints were only so little. If she didn’t look close enough, she would most definitely not have found them.

The Reaper Mantis flew along slowly, following the trail of faint footprints.

She found that there was a faint trail behind her flight path as well.

Although she barely flapped her wings to levitate, the flight path was still rather clear. The Reaper Mantis was lost in thought. Could it be that all the beasts that passed by would leave a trail automatically? It seemed useless to leave traces as it would only expose the target. So why does this happen? The test of the Spirit Beast Great Hall was definitely not useless. There must be some secret that needs to be solved… Cautiously advancing by dozens of meters and turning through a whirling passageway, the Reaper Mantis did not trigger any sort of mechanism nor did she see the Cyclops. What was in front of her, however, were two different choices.

Two corridors’ entrances.

At both entrances, there were even traces of the Cyclops and traces of her flight.

What is going on? Even if I have returned to the original point, there should only be a straight road ahead!

There must be a fake entrance to the corridor.

But which one is fake?

“Huh?” The Reaper Mantis was deep in thought once again.

“Hoho!” She heard the loud roar of the Cyclops resounding throughout the end of the corridor before something black and large was lunged towards her.

The Reaper Mantis instinctively avoided it.

There was a loud boom, originating from a heavy rock that hit the ground in black. Before the Death Mantis could see clearly, another dark thing raced towards her at a faster speed. The place where the giant rock was thrown seemed to be a weighing mechanism. Once it exceeded the weight, it would automatically close the door outside. This thought flashed in the Reaper Mantis’s heart and she immediately reacted, condensing a ball of energy from her little hand.

The energy ball flew towards the second dark object.

The ugly face of the Cyclops that was hidden in the dark flashed a smirk.

This clever little brat is dead. Even if she was careful, no matter how cautious she could be, how could she know of the cruelty that occurs in the test halls if she didn’t get to experience her own fair share of it?

The faint light radiating from the energy ball shot through her hand. The light reflected the hideous face of the Cyclops and also reflected the object of the second black shadow— a wooden box. It was a wooden box that the Cyclops had moved at some point. According to the fragility of the wooden box, not to mention the blow of the energy ball, even a mere sneeze of a Heaven Rank beast would blast it into smithereens. The energy ball flew and hit the wooden box. Seeing the fait accompli that couldn’t be altered, the Cyclops quickly retreated a thousand times faster than its previous step-by-step approach.

Once the wooden box was blasted by the energy ball, the shockwave would definitely trigger those obviously exposed mechanisms.

With the power of the mechanism, and the rules of punishment that came with a damaged box, that little one is an absolute goner!

At the same time, when the Cyclops retreated back violently, it even threw out a dozen wooden boxes that it secretly stored before, all with a precise force.

If more than a dozen wooden boxes exploded together, even with a 30% chance of thunder striking, she would most definitely definitely be hit.

The Cyclops was secretly proud of itself, why would the Toad King specifically choose it to do the killing? The reason was simple— because it was smarter! Who would have ever imagined that under such an ugly and stupid appearance, its schemes and tricks were so thorough. In the familiar Spirit Beast Great Hall, countless newborn beasts have fallen victim to its tricks and tactics.

The Great Hall of Spirit Beasts was simply the best hunting ground!

Boom!

An earth-shattering explosion broke out ahead.

The thermal shockwave of the explosion, as well as a heap of rubble, even impacted on the door of the room where the Cyclops flew back into.

It was a success!

The Cyclops was overjoyed in its heart. After all, who else would dare to use such a high force of an energy ball inside the Spirit Beast Great Hall? She was basically asking for death! It held the urge to go out and see how its opponent died as it stayed in the room.

When the rumbling series of explosions gradually passed, the sound of thunder striking disappeared slowly. It seemed to hear a group of beasts shouting again, so it waited for the voices to dissipate. Only then did the Cyclops leave the room while whistling. It can be concluded that the newborn beast was dead because this was a very simple reasoning. When the wooden box was broken by the energy ball, the shockwave of the explosion not only shattered the dozens of wooden boxes that flew behind, but also triggered those traps that were clearly visible at a glance. The explosive power of the mechanism was already enough to make that little speck of a heavy blow. Plus, there were a dozen boxes with a 30% chance of thunder. As for the group of beasts shouting, it was even simpler. The wooden box that broke was the one where they would get a 50/50 chance in letting the wild beasts of the Beast Valley inside.

As long as the target dies, the beasts that were sent in at will, will be sent away again.

This was why they were quiet for a while.

There was no need to look at all. The Cyclops knew that the cautious little brat was dead…by his own ingenious plan!

In this corridor of mirrors, how many war beasts had it killed? One hundred or two hundred? The Cyclops itself couldn’t remember the exact figure clearly, and now he just wanted to go out as soon as possible, find the Toad King and receive its share of the promised reward— a hundred Heaven Ranked Magic Crystals. If it had that much of a reward, it could break through and improve from the 4th Level to the 5th Level. All this was done so it could survive the Beast Valley, and add a little piece of an extra guarantee.

“Ha ha…”

The Cyclops laughed triumphantly, while inspecting the scene of its painstakingly arranged ‘masterpiece’, enjoying the honour of victory and the pride of its IQ advantage.

Suddenly, its laughter stopped, as if it was cut by a clean sweep of a knife.

An unbelievable sight appeared before its one eye.

The little brat that should be dead, was very well alive. Under her feet, there were a dozen wooden boxes stacked, with all their triggers intact. Except for a huge rock that had been blown to pieces, nothing else had changed.

How can it be?

I clearly saw the energy ball hitting the wooden box!

As if seeing the confusion of the Cyclops, the small face of the human form reincarnated Reaper Mantis, showed a smile that looked like her master Yue Yang.

Her little hand condensed an energy ball that exuded a faint light. Through this glaringly weak light, the Cyclops suddenly had a trace of understanding. Why is this little brat condensing an energy ball? Her main objective was not to attack, but to shed light. Through the light, she had actually seen that the second object it threw was a wooden box, not a huge rock.

Then why would she hit the wooden box with an energy ball?

The Reaper Mantis gave the answer.

With a little finger, a wooden box flew up, floating in the air.

The energy ball flew over and hit the wooden box, but instead of exploding, it caused an occurrence of a refraction. The energy ball made the wooden box hit around the area with extreme speed and bounced like a bouncy ball in the corridor. It was running amok, but not once did it trigger any of the mechanisms.

In the end, the energy ball that had bounced off for a long time returned to the hands of the Reaper Mantis, as if it had never been thrown out.

And now, the Cyclops understood.

It turned out that this energy ball was simply under the control of its opponent.

As long as this little one was unwilling, the ball would do whatever she wanted. It would not explode at all, nor would it break the wooden box.

If she didn’t break the wooden box, where did the sound of the shockwave, gravel and thunder that triggered the mechanism come from? It even heard the roar of a beast just now, and that was most likely not an illusion!

The Reaper Mantis smiled again.

She turned her body upside down and kicked the energy ball that was suspended in mid-air forcefully. The energy ball smashed into a hole in the ground where the giant rock had perched on, and the rubble scattered in front of the Cyclops. The energy ball exploded, causing the shockwave of the explosion to spread. However, like the ripples on the water surface, there will be no impact when encountering any obstacles as only a second ripple will be born, which will spread again. This was the rubble and the shockwave that the Cyclops had seen and sensed respectively. When its dumbfounded gaze looked up, it found that every time the Reaper Mantis flicked her finger, a huge sound of different-sized explosions would be made in the space of her will.

These explosions were also under the control of her will. Although the sound was earth-shattering, they did not produce any impact magically.

The fingers of the Reaper Mantis were connected, and every time they snapped, there would be thunder and lightning that exploded in the energy field.

It was not any sort of punishment thunder of the broken boxes.

As for the roar of the beasts, it was an exquisite ‘Golden Whistle’ from the Reaper Mantis’s mouth. As soon as she took it out and blew it, all kinds of roars and exclamations of various beasts rang one after another!

“This is the Golden Whistle that commands beasts. This is the treasure of the Poisonous Queen Bee, where did you get it?” the Cyclops exclaimed.

“Hehe!” The Reaper Mantis didn’t answer, but her smile made the Cyclops feel like a fool.

The battle between the Beast Kings was always the eternal theme in the Beast Valley.

It turned out to be a chess piece used by the Toad King. No wonder, it would pay a huge reward of one hundred Heaven Ranked Magic Crystals, because it turned out that this was a reward that was never intended to be paid. The Cyclops felt distressed. Between the brawls of the Beast Kings, no matter who won or lost, it will not end well for itself as a chess piece. There was no point in killing this little brat, nor would it matter if she managed to escape— there was no benefit for it.

The Cyclops was about to speak and withdraw from the battle.

Suddenly, it saw the killing intent in the eyes of the Reaper Mantis!

This little thing is angry, this is not good… The Cyclops originally had absolute certainty of killing her, but after the perfect deception of the Reaper Mantis, it discovered that her true strength actually far exceeded its own. Now, she was going to fight it in the Corridor of Mirrors, but the winner was still rather uncertain as anything could happen!

Withdraw!

No matter whether you win or not, there are no positive results.

The Cyclops, who was unwilling to fight, relied on its familiarity with the terrain and quickly bypassed the trap of the mechanism. It raced to the other end of the Corridor of Mirrors.

When it turned around the corner of the corridor, preparing to turn out of the main hall and leave the Spirit Beast Great Hall, it suddenly saw the weird sight of the Reaper Mantis coming in. Why were there two long corridor entrances in front of it? He had obviously left!

Could it be that the little thing saw through my own mechanism operation and reset the roundabout terrain of the long Corridor of Mirrors?

Crack, crack!

When the Cyclops stopped, it heard a crackling sound on the soles of his feet. It lowered its head and realized that at some point, there was a wooden box under its pair of feet. Worst of all, it was broken. The Cyclops jumped up in shock. Snap! It noticed that there was also debris falling on top of its head. When it looked up, it found that there was once a wooden box hanging on it, which it had smashed with its head.

No!

It couldn’t frame the enemy, and yet, the enemy managed to trick it?

When an energy ball hit it, refraction did not occur like when she had hit a wooden box previously. Instead, this ball produced a powerful impact, directly stunning the horrified Cyclops back by three steps. When the Cyclops stabilized, he found himself stepping on the mechanism of a beast’s mouth nozzle.

The flames sprayed out towards the sky right from the middle of the door.

And the Cyclops went blind on the spot.

It was extremely painful. Its huge body was on fire, stumbling and struggling in the corridor. One after another, the traps that were blatantly obvious were all stepped on. Flames, poisonous smoke, lightning and frost— all kinds of traps shot towards the Cyclops as he continued his role of playing a perfect target.

No effort was spared.

At the exit of the corridor, the Reaper Mantis gave this failed-manipulation-Cyclops a very chic silhouette from the back as she smiled warmly. She picked up the Golden Whistle and blew it softly. The monsters that had just been sent in at random, immediately lost their minds when they heard the whistle. Their eyes were red as they all rushed madly at the Cyclops!

========================================================================

chapters.title: Chapter 863 In The Death House, Who Would Win?
chapters.body:
Chapter 863 In The Death House, Who Would Win?

Chapter 863: In The Death House, Who Would Win?

“The little thing has crossed the bridge. It seems like the Cyclops had failed to stop her.”

Hidden in the passage, a dark shadow was waiting for an opportunity to attack.

When it heard the reminder from its accomplice in front, it snorted with disdain, “The one-eyed guy had always proclaimed that it was smart, so it kind of deserved to die. With its small excuse of cleverness, once its plans were seen through, it simply could not walk on any other path but the road to its demise. The ‘Corridor of Mirrors’ wasn’t exactly a place to kill either. In this Spirit Beast Great Hall, taking actions alone would be the most deadly move. Keep your eyes open— when the little thing comes over, I will lure her into the ‘Death House’. Once I come out, trigger the mechanisms.”

The accomplice in front nodded immediately, “Understood, we have cooperated with this ambush tactic for almost 100 times,it won’t be a problem”

The Reaper Mantis flew over.

The shadow ambushing in the darkness flashed, its terrifyingly huge mouth spraying a jet of dark green venom.

There would be smoke and corrosion wherever the venom was spewed on.

But the sneak attack was ineffective, allowing the cautious Reaper Mantis to easily escape.

“Orb!”

The Reaper Mantis evaded the sneak attack and threw an energy ball.

Through the faint light of the energy ball, she realised that there was a long and thin black shadow that went into a passageway.

Not knowing what kind of mechanism had been triggered, the box on the start of the bridge was moved away, causing the part of the bridge behind the Reaper Mantis to be quickly retracted. It seemed like there were also mechanisms that could be manipulated and they would have to compete to earn the controlling rights. Otherwise, it would be impossible to go back. The Reaper Mantis thought for a moment before she followed it. She trailed behind the thin black shadow up front, chasing in pursuit as she went into the passage. About a minute later, there was another noise. The broken bridge that had been retracted now rumbled, extending the rails for reconnection as it stood up once more.

The one who stayed out of pursuit was overjoyed when it saw the success of the plan.

As long as the mechanism of the Death House was reset, the door could be closed.

And once the door is closed, the creatures in the Death House there will fall into the death trap, where they would perish from existence!

It flew to the control handle of the mechanism that closed the door and waited anxiously for its companion to come out. According to the plan, its alliance would come out within ten seconds after the bridge mechanism was reset, whereas the opponent would be slower by a few seconds or maybe even ten. At last, the opponent would be stuck inside the Death House.

As long as it gets to pull down the control handle, the task issued by the Toad King would be considered complete.

Ten, nine, eight… Normally, after seven or eight seconds, its companion would be able to come out. It didn’t know why its friend was a bit slower today.

The unexpected change made the guy who stayed outside the door very anxious.

At the door, a long and thin shadow flashed by.

The anxious guy recognized that this was its companion and it immediately pulled down the control handle forcefully. In an instant, the door of the Death House was closed. The death trap inside was activated, the creatures left in it would eventually turn into a pile of bones from the flames, where even the weaker bodies would not leave behind any form of buone residue. Seeing its companion return safely, the guy who stayed behind couldn’t help complaining, “I’ll say, Withered Snake, can you be just a little bit more serious? That was very unclear, I almost thought the plan had failed just now.”

The Withered Snake who lured the Reaper Mantis into the Death House laughed loudly, “How is that possible, I am a well-trained veteran, after all. It’s just the little one was too cautious so I had to lure her farther inside. I did keep my promise to complete it within my allocated time, though.”

“Anyway, don’t make it so susceptible next time!” The guy who stayed behind was still slightly dissatisfied.

“Okay, Big-Eyed Monkey, stop complaining, I’ll give you one more Heaven Ranked Magic Crystal, you’ll get 11 and I’ll take 19. How’s that, are you satisfied now?” The Withered Snake spat at the nonexistent blackmailing, basking in the remnants of the aura in the atmosphere, “There are no traces of auras outside and the surroundings are safe— our plan was 100% successful. Now, let’s get some wreckage and give it to the Toad King. I hope that little thing will have some bone remnants left to make sure the Toad King doesn’t think that we’re fooling it! By the way, the one-eyed fool, how can he believe that the Toad King would give out such a huge reward like 100 Heaven Ranked Magic Crystals? You know, I think just offering 30 Magic Crystals is already the most generous thing that the Toad King has done in its entire lifetime.”

“There is indeed no one around, but don’t we need to wait? Maybe that little thing is very tough and it hasn’t died yet!” The guy who stayed behind just now said worriedly.

“Don’t worry about it, there is no such beast from the Beast Valley who can survive in the Death House! Even if the Toad King enters, it will become a dead toad in a few seconds. But it’s a pity that it wasn’t willing to go in even if you force it to its death. Otherwise, I will send it on the road to the afterlife long ago!” The Withered Snake chuckled, using the control handle to open the door of the Death House.

A scorching stench of the burnt corpses spreaded out.

The Withered Snake and the Big-Eyed Monkey looked at each other and smiled.

Usually, only the beasts that they cooperated to kill would emit this kind of musky smell. If there were none, that would have been odd.

They took a deep breath and Withered Snake laughed brazenly, “The authentic smell of burning corpses— my favorite!”

When the bridge was disconnected again, it was impossible for newcomers to enter, and no one would be able to disturb them. The two beasts with no worries walked side by side into the Dead House, acting together and trusting each other, which was the basis of cooperation between them. Usually, they would take turns to draw lots and lure the enemy. As the party who committed the risk, they will get more shares of the reward. In the process of collecting the corpse as evidence, the two parties agree that they must participate together to prevent someone from intending to take advantage of it afterwards. To prevent others from trying to steal that advantage of theirs, they triggered the mechanism.

It was precisely because the Withered Snake and Big-eyed Monkey cooperated happily and trusted each other that they were able to eradicate almost 100 enemies using the mechanisms of the Death House and the tacit understanding between them despite not being very strong themselves.

The Big-Eyed Monkey carried an old and dirty corpse bag in its hand.

In addition to collecting a little bit of evidence for the exchange, the corpses in the Death House must be thoroughly cleaned up before the next prey can be fooled.

Otherwise, the corpses in the Death House would pile up.

And by then, even fools would also be suspicious!

“It’s strange, why are there no corpses? Were they all incinerated?” The Big-Eyed Monkey searched for a long time, but it didn’t find any corpses.

“What’s so strange about this? The little thing was already small originally, so the bones must have been burnt and that was absolutely possible. Perhaps it might be the flame storm that blew the residues to another corner or something. You must know that when the door of the Death House is closed, the flames inside would sweep up everything and anything. I remember once, two mammoths came and their bone residues had also been blown thousands of meters away by the flame storm. Let’s just search carefully for it. If we truly can’t find it, then we’ll just forget it!” The Withered Snake didn’t care. It saw with its own eyes that the little thing was behind him up until the doors of the Death House were closed. She didn’t have time to leave and she had no choice but to stay behind.

Rumble!

A familiar sound rang from a distance.

The Withered Snake and the Big-Eyed Monkey looked at each other in confusion. What’s going on? It sounded like the sound of the bridge being reconnected.

This is bad. Once the bridge is reconnected, the control handle outside the Death House door can be pulled down.

If someone pulls down the handle now, the doors of the Death House will close.

By that time, the consequences would be disastrous… The Big-Eyed Monkey and the Withered Snake were no longer adamant to look for any more corpses as they scampered towards the exit frantically.

When the two of them rushed to the area where the bridge-controlling handle was located inside the Death House, they were horrified to find the Reaper Mantis waiting there with a smile. She held a sweet gaze as if she was looking at an old friend. She even waved her hands affectionately, as if to express something along the lines of ‘l haven’t seen you in forever’ and they also seemed to be asking them to hurry up.

“Stop her!” Both the Withered Snake and the Big-Eyed Monkey understood what it meant to pull the bridge handle down.

If this little thing went out to pull down the control handle of the Death House’s door, any life trapped inside would turn into ashes!

The Withered Snake and the Big-Eyed Monkey exhausted all their strength to launch attacks on the Reaper Mantis.

However, their random attacks all failed to hit their true target. The afterimage flashed and the Reaper Mantis’s shadow disappeared, where her true body appeared at the door of the Death House three kilometers away. If she hadn’t condensed a faintly glowing energy ball in her hands, the Withered Snake and the Big-Eyed Monkey wouldn’t have found out about her existence yet. Whether it was the Withered Snake or the Big-Eyed Monkey, they were both frightened. They never thought that the door of the Death House would become their own door to Hell one day!

Even if they tried their best to sprint, using an enormous effort to sprint towards safety, the slower Big-Eyed Monkey became anxious. It knew that if it fell behind, it would undoubtedly die.

It took action very quickly as it pulled the snake’s tail with one hand, hoping that its companion could bring him along. Even if it was just making it go a little bit faster, it was better than nothing.

However, the Withered Snake was angry. It wanted to speed up and escape from the Death House, but now the Big-Eyed Monkey was dragging him down. It did not hesitate. While flying, its tail flicked heavily, slapping the Big-Eyed Monkey on the ground and took advantage of its force to eject itself forward at high speed. With the help of the counter-shock of its companion’s body, the speed of the Withered Snake increased by a few notches.

Three more seconds and it believed that it could escape from the Death House.

Three, two… When it was merely 100 meters away from the door of the Death House, the doors closed instantly as the Reaper Mantis smiled and waved goodbye.

The Withered Snake slammed itself against the door desperately. It was only a second away, and things were only a door apart, but it was a difference between Heaven and Earth.

A difference between life and death.

A storm of fire spurted out and swept across the Death House.

“No, ah no!”

The Withered Snake and Big-Eyed Monkey who murdered nearly a hundred beasts with this trick, howled miserably as they turned into ashes in the flames. Before the door’s control handle, there was a flea-like monster with an ugly mouth grinning. The tiny eyes showed a hint of pleasure from revenge.

“The task assigned to this servant by the Poisonous Queen Bee has been completed. Because of your trust and cooperation, the servant’s greatest urge for vengeance has been avenged. The two big enemies, Withered Snake and the Big-Eyed Monkey, have also turned into ashes. With that, this servant will take its leave. For the next levels, please keep working hard!” The flea respectfully saluted the Reaper Mantis before it bounced on the bridge repetitively, quickly leaving the Spirit Beast Great Hall.

“I’ll work hard!” The Reaper Mantis waved goodbye to the flea who had come to help her. After successfully killing the Withered Snake and the Big-Eyed Monkey, she encouraged herself to continue to the second and third levels.

She wanted to get the best sign, even if she couldn’t get any help.

However, for those who are smart, sometimes the enemy is your best ‘companion’ and the enemy would be no less helpful than an actual companion.

You must continue to work hard, you are the best… The Reaper Mantis, who was reborn as a human, clenched her small fist adorably as she flew to the next level with an expression full of determination. Although the test was difficult as it was filled with various mechanisms that could have been fatally dangerous, those traits weren’t able to scare her. Besides, why wouldn’t this Spirit Beast Great Hall be the perfect place for her to train and grow?

========================================================================

========================================================================

It’s been a month since I’ve started tl-ing LLS, so it’s not unfamiliar territory (not much anyway, still some confusion though) but please do let me know if there were terms that I have used incorrectly like the names etc. Thanks and look forward to the release!

-Sterling
