chapters.title: Chapter 878 The Generous And Magnanimous Third Young Master
chapters.body:
Chapter 878 The Generous And Magnanimous Third Young Master

Chapter 878: The Generous And Magnanimous Third Young Master

In the tavern of White Stone City.

Because this was the place where the Third Young Master of the Yue Clan rose up in the ranks, the tavern here in White Stone City had once received a reward from the Warrior Guild. It was completely renovated and then, there was a room that was renovated into a martial arts square. In the middle of the square, there stood a statue of a juvenile flying an eagle. The statue implied that this is the place where the number one warrior of the Tong Tian Tower, Third Young Master of the Yue Clan, had started to soar.

There were not only many mercenaries from the Soaring Dragon Continent and young people with dreams who came to the White Stone City for sightseeing, even the several allied races of the Tong Tian Tower, such as dwarves, tauren, pigmen, golden elves, etc were here. There were many people who came here under the leadership of the Tong Tian Tower’s martial artists, all of them wanting to see the real location that symbolised the rise of the Third Young Master of the Yue Clan.

The soil in White Stone City was said to be a popular souvenir among all the tribes of the Tong Tian Tower.

Giving the other party a piece of White Stone City’s soil meant wishing the other party’s future generations to take off into the road of success.

Had it not been for Jun Wu You’s strict order to ban the digging of dirt, the houses that Yue Yang and Si-niang lived in would have most definitely been demolished.

“Really? A mercenary like me who has nothing but a pathetic life can also sign up for the wedding of the Third Young Masters? What? What did you say? Those who pass the test of the Dragon Knights Legion Army can get a Gold-ranked flying dragon and could even join the Dragon Knights Legion Army of the Third Young Master?” There was a one-eyed mercenary. He and his companions had just returned from a mission. When they entered the tavern, they immediately trembled with excitement. The wine in their hands spilled on the table without them noticing.

“It’s like a dream!” The mercenaries around them couldn’t believe their ears.

“Why would I, Gold Teeth, ever lie? Do you think that I’d earn some form of money if I lied to you? Besides, do you know what kind of person is the Third Young Master? So many people kept saying that he was more important than the imperial edict— you can suspect anyone else, but you must not question the Third Young Master. Besides, you can’t appreciate the greatness and kindness of the Third Young Master , which is why you had such dirty thoughts to question him. Fortunately, you are here— if you were to be in the capital, you would have been beaten up the moment you spoke badly about the Third Young Master!” There was an old mercenary with two golden teeth that was proudly sitting on a chair at the moment. He, who is usually a scumbag, now enjoys the honor of the tavern owner pouring wine for him and entertaining him. He was the one who relayed the news of the Third Young Master getting married and the formation of the Dragon Knights Legion Army, after all.

“I’m not questioning the Third Young Master, I wouldn’t dare to. I just couldn’t react properly,” The one-eyed mercenary quickly pleaded. It wasn’t really a joke; the crime of insulting the Third Young Master was not something that he could bear!

“I guessed that you wouldn’t dare to!” Golden Teeth laughed loudly, “Otherwise, I will use this old life of mine to fight you to the end.”

“Yes,” The strength of the one-eyed mercenary was more than ten times higher than that of Golden Teeth.

But, because he bore the news from the Third Young Master, the current Golden Teeth was simply sacred and inviolable.

Hence, the one-eyed mercenary apologized over and over again.

When the old mercenary Golden Teeth saw that the people in the tavern were attracted by the news he had brought, he felt very proud as everyone hovered around him.

He had even intentionally delivered the news at a calm and steady pace. He slowly picked up a crispy peanut with his chopsticks and chewed it tastefully, ignoring the curious mercenaries who were eager to know more about the news as they coaxed him repetitively.

He waited until the necks of the mercenaries were all tightened from the suspense before he slammed his chopsticks hard on the table, vividly saying, “Do you think that my name Golden Teeth was given for nothing? My news is definitely the latest and the most accurate! News in the White Stone City was always spreaded last; if we were in the big cities of the capital, it would have spreaded long ago… Think about it, can the betrothal gifts of the Third Young Master be that simple? What kind of person is the Third Young Master? Hehe, he prepared for this for such a long time, he must have definitely hosted a grand ceremony! I’ll say, you don’t know everything yet, because if you do, you would be shocked to death on the spot? The news that had been scrounged by this Golden Teeth is a mere bit. However, that mere bit of information is enough to make me tremble!”

“What the hell is the Third Young Master’s betrothal gift? Tell us about it!” The one-eyed mercenary poured wine to the Golden Teeth out of flattery.

“The part that cannot be said is a state secret, which is definitely not something that people like us have the right to know of. Even those who hold the power of the upper-ranks or the relatives and friends of the Third Young Master know of a part of the whole story. The most secretive part was that the Third Young Master had communicated secretly with both of His Majesties,” Golden Teeth had a glass of wine. His face was flushed as he patted the table very proudly, “If I had not accepted the task, I would not dare to talk of such nonsense. Do you know who sent me on this mission to deliver these messages?”

“Is it the Third Young Master?” The mercenaries were all excited when they heard it.

“In your dreams. The Third Young Master is a busy person and he is already piled up with marriage; where would he ever have the time to post some tasks for me? Besides, doing this kind of thing would not be in line with his noble status!” Golden Teeth sneered and despised the mercenary’s mediocre wisdom.

“Yes, yes, we were just stunned for a while,” The people in the tavern laughed. Indeed, the Third Young Master didn’t know Golden Teeth at all, so how could he ever issue a mission to him!

“The person who issued the task to me was the mentor of the Third Young Master. Speaking of the name, you must know that it is Master Xia Hou Wei Jie, the most famous eagle-eyed man in the Soaring Dragon Continent!” Golden Teeth wore a reminiscent expression, “Just yesterday, Master Xia Hou gathered all the mercenaries in the capital’s Mercenary Guild, which included me, and invited us to dinner. Then, he released an impromptu task of having us spread the news of the marriage of the Third Young Master. Before this White Stone City, I had already talked about this good news in four different cities. What? You want to know their reactions? It was no less than sensational! All the listeners were reluctant to let me go. If the task didn’t require forcing me to act, I wouldn’t have come here as I would already be overwhelmed by the mercenaries of the Red Forest City. Well, poor mercenaries, the dawn of your hope is here, and that is the greatest and kindest Third Young Master had officialised that if any of you can enter the Dragon Knights Legion Army or the Dragon Blood Guard Legion, don’t forget to be grateful for this gift from the Third Young Master!”

“Tell us! Tell us in detail!” The one-eyed mercenary and a few companions tidied up the table, and ordered the tavern owner to bring up a plate of the most famous Charcoal-fired Demon Achyranthes, all for Golden Teeth to enjoy alone.

“What about people like us who have no potential and are in their thirties? Our mission failed and we became paupers. I am afraid that we can’t even pay the registration fee for the Dragon Knights Legion Army!” There was another mercenary with a beard who sighed with worry.

In fact, there were many people who thought like him.

The mercenaries were all people who gambled their own blood for their livelihoods. They don’t know when they will die, so they spend money as soon as they get rich, gambling wildly with little to no savings.

Let’s not talk about the difficulty of the Dragon Knights Legion Army test yet.

They feared that they couldn’t even pay for the registration fees for the test.

“Pui!” Golden Teeth suddenly became furious as he pointed at the bearded mercenary’s nose and cursed, “How can you use such dirty thoughts to second-guess the kindness of the Third Young Master? Who said anything about a registration fee? Why would the Third Young Master want money from you lousy mercenaries who were worse than beggars? He just has to say one word and someone will pave a carpet of gold coins and let him walk on it. Who said that there is an application fee? No, one is not needed as it is totally free!”

“Completely free?” The mercenaries were dumbfounded as this was the first time they have heard of such thing

There is no free work in this world.

If this was truly free of charge, that would basically mean that the expenses would be handled by the Third Young Master himself.

In order to form this Dragon Knights Legion Army, the Third Young Master had already provided more opportunities and Flying Dragons for them, and yet he didn’t even charge a single cent for the registration fee?

When the mercenaries thought about it, they couldn’t help but feel a little embarrassed and angry at themselves. It was indeed much too disrespectful for them to use traditional ideologies to depict the Third Young Master. Imagining a noble person such as the Third Young Master, who was naturally the most generous person in the world, as a greedy vampire of wealth was definitely a tad too overboard— no wonder Golden Teeth got so agitated!

“Ah, I was wrong, I really didn’t know it was free. Besides, I didn’t intend to disrespect the Third Young Master, I just thought too much about it…” The mercenaries who blundered gave themselves a slap on the face and some of them apologised profusely, implying that they now knew of the generosity of the Third Young Master and would not ever badmouth him again.

“People with no potential or people like us who are of a senior age can’t enter the Dragon Knights Legion Army, so what should I do?” Someone asked.

“Listen. In fact, Lord Xia Hou told us a long time ago— no matter what your current strength is, the Third Young Master won’t look down on you and your innate rank does not matter in the eyes of the Third Young Master! No matter how bad you are, the Third Young Master will not dislike you; he is the greatest as there is no one else who does it like him. He knows that even if you’re not that apt in cultivation, it does not mean that you have no potential. The Third Young Master has his own ways of doing things as he has a method that could measure a person’s potential most accurately. Of course, you guys should keep expectations low— if your strength does not have potential, don’t be too disappointed. After all, anyone who is of a slightly older age might have used up your potential long ago, so it’s normal to be inferior to the younger generations… If you can’t enter the Dragon Knights Legion Army, your last hope would be to enter the Dragon Blood Guard Legion,” Golden Teeth firmly affirmed the greatness of the Third Young Master of the Yue Clan.

“Dragon Blood Guard Legion?” The mercenaries heard it as their eyes glimmered brightly.

“This Dragon Blood Guard Legion— to be precise, is both the easiest and the most difficult to enter,” Golden Teeth replied.

“How is it the easiest and the hardest?” The mercenaries in the tavern looked at each other in confusion. Isn’t that contradictory?

“The easy part is, it is said that anyone can enter and this door is always open for everyone. They’re always recruiting, so you can always be hopeful,” Golden Teeth first raised his left hand, and then raised his right hand to strengthen the power of his speech. “Speaking of the hard part is really the hardest… According to Master Xia Hou, the Third Young Master hopes to give all the warriors in the Soaring Dragon Continent a chance. Remember, it is open to everyone, including you and me here. If there is someone among us who can pass the test of the will, no matter what the strength is, there will be hope to join the Dragon Blood Guard Legion! The requirements are completely different from the potential and the intelligence of the Dragon Knights Legion Army because the Dragon Blood Guard legion requires only one thing— and that is persistence. Master Xia Hou said that the Third Young Master once stipulated that even if a worthless mediocre was willing to do one thing for ten years, as long as he or she has a firm will, then they can join the Dragon Blood Guard Legion. This is because as long as one’s willpower is recognised, the Third Young Master can bestow the Dragon Blood Pill to that mediocre person and immediately make them stronger!”

As soon as this was said, the entire tavern was silent.

Everyone held their breath.

The suppressed breathing and gulping sounds of the mercenaries were particularly loud in the tavern.

It took a long time before the mercenaries came back to their senses, practically when Golden Teeth was gnawing on the Charcoal-fired Demon Achyranthes.

Before Golden Teeth left, he dropped another bombshell of news, “The number of the Dragon Knights Legion Army has been reserved for three thousand, but the total number of elites from all the families in the Soaring Dragon Continent is less than three hundred. In other words, every single one of you has a chance; even your children or a three-year-old child can take the test. Maybe, one of them will be a future Dragon Knight! Everyone, I have done my part in bringing this news to your attention. There is no catch as long as you always remember one thing— the Third Young Master is the greatest. You must always remember to be grateful and don’t forget your roots!”

The mercenaries were still flabbergasted even when Golden Teeth left the tavern to continue his journey of spreading the news to the neighbouring cities.

This…Was this all a dream?

Was it true?

This shocking news had turned the Soaring Dragon Continent into a sensational atmosphere of uproar.

The Third Young Master of the Yue Clan formally courted the ladies of the Xue Family, the Da Xia Royal Family and the Tian Luo Family of the Four Major Families. The three families had also agreed to the marriage contract of the Yue Clan as they betrothed the First Miss of the Xue Family, Princess Qian Qian of the Da Xia Royal Family and City Lord Luo Hua of the Tian Luo Family to him. Among them, Miss Xue of the Xue Family would be the main wife, while Princess Qian Qian and City Lord Luo Hua would both be side wives.

The marriage news was enough to shock the world, but the promise made by the Third Young Master of the Yue Clan when he was courting the three ladies made the world overturn in chaos.

The original words of the Third Young Master were as follows:

“In order to show my respect to the Xue Family, the Da Xia Royal Family and the Tian Luo Family, I am willing to form a Dragon Knights Legion Army to take the lead and wed the brides.

This Third Young Master of the Yue Clan promises to recruit members for the Dragon Knights Legion Army to face the entire Soaring Dragon Continent, even if they are allies of the Tong Tian Tower.

The additional point is that as long as they can pass the test of potential or the test of will, no matter what strength, identity and age they are, anyone can apply to join the reserve team of the Dragon Knights Legion Army.

Once entered, in addition to receiving the best training, they will also be granted with a Flying Dragon of a strength level that was no lower than the Level 5 Innate Golden Rank waiting to be contracted.

All to ensure that any ordinary mortal can be a Dragon Knight in no time.

If the news is not surprising enough, everyone would be in for a shock later.

Because, there is no need to despair even if the test applicants do not meet the requirements for the Dragon Knights Legion Army.

The most benevolent, most generous, greatest, and most praised Third Young Master of the Yue Clan will also promise a condition that will definitely touch everyone’s hearts.

As long as the tester’s willpower is strong enough, even if their abilities are subpar, the Third Young Master will give them an opportunity. Even if they can’t join the Dragon Knights Legion Army, they can join the Dragon Blood Guard Legion and become a glorious Dragon Blood Warrior!”

This was the promise made by the Third Young Master of the Yue Clan when he was courting the three ladies, a decision that was recognized to change the destiny of the entire Soaring Dragon Continent!

When the world shed tears and admired the Third Young Master, they would never know how he had shamelessly defrauded the Double-Headed Black Dragon in the Beast Valley and how he had greedily forced the Dragon Whistle, blood, teeth, claw, horns, scales, Dragon Flame, Star Stones and Moon Gems. Now that the Soaring Dragon Continent was in a roaring sensation, no one would believe it even if someone told the truth.

Because, in the eyes of ordinary warriors, the Third Young Master of the Yue Clan was synonymous with generosity and magnanimity.

He was a sacred and inviolable existence!

[TL note]:

New update!! Will do a new post a new chp once every two or three days, as it’s the more relaxed schedule for October so thanks for understanding!

At this point there’s more new characters that are emerging so I’m kind of making the glossary instead of referring to it. I’ll try my best to provide them as accurately as I can, so let me know if any of the names seem awkward or don’t flow all that well!

At any rate, enjoy the chapter! Hope you guys have a great day/night ahead of you!

-Sterling

chapters.title: Chapter 879 His Majesty'S Research? Crazy Ideas
chapters.body:
Chapter 879 His Majesty'S Research? Crazy Ideas

Chapter 879: His Majesty’s Research? Crazy Ideas

[T/L Note]:

Before we get into the real chapter, I would like to know, dear avid readers of LLS, is Hua Xu Ri— the Emperor of the Tian Luo Empire, a woman all this time??? Since I picked this up at chapter 817, I wasn’t aware of the genders but just assumed it was a guy all this time. Previous translations have labeled the Emperor as a ‘he’ and the ‘brother’ of the Eastern Sky King, but on the Wiki Fandom page, the alias was ‘Princess Night Dream’ and her gender stated was a female.

At any rate, I’ll address the Emperor as a man for now but will change it if said otherwise. Author didn’t use the gender prefixes so I can’t really make sense of it through this chapter alone, so I’d appreciate it if anyone can just let me know in the comments, thank you!!

Now, back to the story.

The Tian Luo Palace.

What surprised Yue Yang was the fact that His Majesty had abruptly summoned him today.

Usually he would refuse to see me under the pretext of being unwell even when I request an audience with him, so what’s going on today?

Yue Yang was confused, but he was also brimming with expectation— after all, His Majesty isn’t someone that you could see whenever you wanted to. The on-duty female officer led him to the steps of the inner hall before she quietly retreated, a door separating him from His Majesty just like the last time. If it was someone else who treated Yue Yang like this, it would be strange if he didn’t directly kick the hall door and rush in. However, the trick of playing the role of a rogue wouldn’t work in His Majesty’s palace. Here, he had to pretend to be a good boy.

So, he kept on smiling.

The transversing man wore a polite and serene expression.

“I heard that you, Jun Wu You and Shui Dong Liu have formed a Dragon Knights Legion Army which has led to the evoked martial spirit of the entire Soaring Dragon Continent?” His Majesty asked.

“I’m actually not that magnanimous…” Student Yue Yang was humble on the surface, but he was very proud in his heart. If the method that he had painstakingly thought about had no effect at all, it would be strange!

Yue Yang took out a plan script and walked up the steps, as if he wanted to open the door of the hall and enter, but in the end he didn’t.

Instead, he used a kind of ‘Telekinesis Transmission’ to pass the plan into the inner hall and said in a serious tone,” Several seniors, as well as Mr. Nan Gong, all believe that if the Soaring Dragon Continent wants to rise, it must remain competitive. Searching for elites to form the Dragon Knights Legion Army and using the honors and opportunities to inspire the nation’s martial arts spirit is just the first step.”

“The plan is pretty good, but I don’t know how effective it will be,” His Majesty pondered for a long time, seeming to read the plan thoroughly and carefully before he finally made a point, “I agree a bit more towards the latter idea of forming the Dragon Blood Guard Legion. It’s impossible for everyone to be an elite— some are talented people, some have strong willpower and most importantly, some of them are loyal. Indeed, it would be possible for them to develop some opportunities that differ with individuality so that they can excel in their respective fields. Many predecessors have thought about this matter, but in the end, no one did it well, including the Prison Emperor. If you have decided to do it, then I will still support it. I can only hope that you will do your best to the very end.”

“With Your Majesty’s order, this student will naturally go all out!” Student Yue Yang smiled and bowed.

“I won’t participate in the plan of the Dragon Knights Legion and the Dragon Blood Guard,” His Majesty paused before saying, “With your abilities and the cooperation of everyone, I believe it can be done even if it takes a longer period of time. After all, with your reputation, it would be a bit difficult to motivate the drive of the entire Tong Tian Tower, but it would not be a big problem against the Soaring Dragon Continent.”

“Don’t! Your Majesty, don’t let go of this matter!” Yue Yang was anxious as what he had hoped for was the full support of His Majesty.

“To have the Eastern Sky King and the Western Sky King cooperate with you is really impossible; I will send over a special envoy to take charge of this matter,” His Majesty seems to have decided long ago.

“Then what are you busy with all day? You stay so idly in the harem every day, after all— isn’t it boring not to do something?” Yue Yang sweated profusely.

“It’s none of your business!” His Majesty was angry.

“Just think of it as a small favor,” Yue Yang hurriedly expressed his sincere attitude.

“I have been very busy recently and I have no time at all!” His Majesty said so, although no one in the Soaring Dragon Continent would believe his words. Everyone knew that Tian Luo Emperor Hua Xu Ri was the most leisurely Emperor in the world. He stayed in the harem every day and rarely ever showed up to court meetings. He almost never asked about government affairs, leaving all domestic affairs up to the ministers’ judgements.

There was a joke in the Soaring Dragon Continent that there was once a major celebration held in Tian Luo Square. His Majesty Hua Xu Ri was slightly ill and it was too late for him to get dressed formally. Hence, the female officer took him out of the palace and prepared to go to the square to participate in the celebration. As a result, they were stopped by the official responsible for maintaining order as the official asked for the VIP invitation from His Majesty. When the female officer showed the Sunburst Jade Pendant that proved His Majesty’s identity, the official thought it was a bribe and strictly refused, “For His Majesty’s safety, this official absolutely can’t allow any unidentified people to enter the square, so please leave immediately. Otherwise, this official will call the palace guards to arrest you…” This joke might have been a bit exaggerated as it would be impossible for the official responsible for maintaining order to not recognise the Emperor’s ‘Sunburst Jade Pendant’.

It was even more improbable for the Tian Luo Empire to let a ‘blind’ official, who did not recognize His Majesty, take on the responsibility for maintaining order.

However, when it comes to reality.

The situation was pretty much like this.

In the Tian Luo Empire, at least one-fifth of the ministers and one-tenth of the City Lords have never seen the Emperor Hua Xu Ri. It was said that there were more than one hundred Tian Luo officials who often mistook the representative official, the Guardian War God, as the Emperor instead. This shows how much the country’s citizens weren’t exposed to Emperor Hua Xu Ri’s face as the king of the country.

Compared with the Da Xia Emperor Jun Wu You, the Tian Luo Emperor Hua Xu Ri was really low-key and outrageous.

Although Jun Wu You never printed his head on gold coins to make everyone recognise his face, at least 80% of the people, including civilians, were aware of what their Emperor looked like in Da Xia.

Especially with the rise of student Yue Yang, he had indirectly led Jun Wu You to become famous all over the world. Even some of the people in the Tong Tian Tower had heard of his glorious name.

When Yue Yang heard His Majesty say that she was busy, he couldn’t help but laugh in secret.

He thought, if you are very busy, then there would no longer be any idlers in the world.

His Majesty seemed to have seen Yue Yang’s heartfelt voice and he whispered, “I really am busy. If you don’t know, don’t try to guess!”

Yue Yang was surprised, “Then what is Your Majesty up to?”

His Majesty groaned for a long time before she let out a sigh, “I’m researching if there is a way to replace the Grimoire with something else!”

“Isn’t this rather whimsical?” Yue Yang was obviously not optimistic. If it were Jun Wu You or the old fox who said this, Yue Yang would treat them as insane; replacing the Grimoire is a really idiotic move after all. If there was anything that could replace the Grimoire, why should the martial artists of the Heaven Realm look at someone else’s Grimoire and drool? Yue Yang, with his current ability of the Heaven Eyes Transverse, was unable to see any traces of the creation nor the truth. He guessed that even the legendary Gods would not be able to create the Grimoires. Only the Divine Gods of the ancient times that were incomparably strong might be able to do something like that.

Now, His Majesty Hua Xu Ri, the Emperor of Tian Luo, said that he was studying something that may be capable of replacing the Grimoire.

Yue Yang thought that he had already been mindful of His Majesty’s reputation as he hadn’t laughed out loud till his death.

The two faced each other, both speechless for a while, not knowing how long it took before His Majesty suddenly sighed slightly, “The Grimoires were too mysterious. There have been people of the Heaven Stairway who have studied it in the past dynasties, but they have not made significant progress. Later, the people of the Heaven Stairway changed their minds and put forward a bold idea— even if they failed to accomplish a world without the presence of the Grimoires, would they be able to use something else that could essentially replace the Grimoire itself?”

Yue Yang was shocked, were these strong Heaven Stairway martial artists too leisured?

There really was such a crazy research idea going on? Listening to His Majesty’s tone, this kind of research has probably been going on for a long time. But even though they know that it was futile, why do they insist on continuing it?

With a little bit of temptation paired with the slightest form of curiosity, Yue Yang asked, “How long did this research last?”

His Majesty replied, “Eight thousand years.”

Yue Yang was speechless.

To spend eight thousand years studying a project that will never succeed— was this persistence or foolishness?

“Like I said, I don’t have time to take care of your business. In the past few months, I have been wanting to sleep. Go back. If necessary, you can find the Eastern Sky King,” His Majesty was going to send Yue Yang away.

“Wait, let me help you!” Yue Yang’s heart decided. If he could make a breakthrough in this aspect, then he believed that it would be of great help towards his comprehension of the realm.

“You don’t have such patience…” His Majesty refused.

“Let’s talk about your ideas and progress first,” Yue Yang insisted on listening, only giving up if it was truly impossible.

“Since eight thousand years ago, a fixed line of thought has gradually formed. We hope to connect and communicate souls through an item, and at the same time, this item will be the carrier of the contracted beast. You probably know as well that the contract beast’s carriers are very common in treasures, especially treasures that were above the Gold Level, such as Holy Rnaked and Divine Ranked treasures, which have instrument spirits. We were wondering if there was a way to meet the requirements of this carrier with the quality being lowered. It is impossible for ordinary warriors to use treasures above the Gold Level and some young children do not have enough spiritual control. If we can research and materialise a carrier of the Silver Level or the Bronze Level that can contract a beast above the Gold Level all the same, isn’t that…” His Majesty talked about this kind of research as his interest suddenly peaked, explaining to Yue Yang endlessly.

“That’s it!” Yue Yang felt that in the inheritance of knowledge, there was also a part of the sealed knowledge about this, but it was incomplete. He guessed that Si-nang had also studied the substitutions of the Grimoires before, but the limited time was not enough for her to study deeper into it.

“The carrier of the contract beast is the first problem. However, the most difficult thing is the communication of the soul,” His Majesty emanated a long sigh, “This is too difficult.”

“Soul communication…” Yue Yang also frowned.

For those who are stronger than the Innate Realm, this poses a problem.

However, for an ordinary person, communicating with the soul could only be an extravagant hope.

Perhaps in a deep dream, there were very few people with pure hearts that would smoothly communicate through the soul under the influence of certain talents or abilities.

Even if ordinary people allowed others to forcibly guide and communicate with their souls, it would also cause damage to their souls. If a carrier with a beast of Gold Rank or higher was allowed to communicate with the soul, it is estimated that the will of that beast will directly destroy the fragile soul of ordinary people.

There was no other way but to summon the incomparable treasure of the Grimoire to bear the burden.

If one really wanted to contract a Battle-type Beast on their souls, they must fully integrate the soul and the body so that the beast and the master are ‘completely one’.

In other words, if one could achieve this, then this beast will be one that guards their lives, with them dying if their owner dies. An ordinary contract with the body would result in the betrayal of the beast should it not be satisfied. Or, if there was no communication of will, the beast will never learn to progress without wisdom.

“Just mind your own business. Just maybe, this path might have been wrong,” His Majesty advised Yue Yang not to put his energy on this.

“What if we make another Grimoire?” Yue Yang fell into deep thought.

There was a kind of aura in his mind now.

Although he didn’t know what it was, he had a strange feeling that if he grasped this, he might have a better understanding of soul communication.

His Majesty felt a little strange, what does this kid want to do?

Does he really want to help her find a substitute for the Grimoire? Has he gone bonkers?

Yue Yang sat on the steps without an image as he was deep in his thoughts, intending to seize the floating inspiration.

Finally, lying on the steps with both hands as a pillow, casually crossing his legs, he stared at the sky with mindless eyes. His mind was focused on plunging into the sea of thoughts— whether it was the inheritance of Si-niang’s knowledge, Empress Fei Wen Li’s Complete Comprehension of the Realms, the wisdom and enlightenment brought to him by all the martial artists of the World Tree and finally the various and diverse knowledge that he had learned of before crossing worlds.

What could be used as a substitute for the Grimoire?

What kind of existence was necessary to achieve this?

His Majesty was afraid that he would interrupt his thoughts, hence he had not spoken and waited quietly with a glimmer of expectation in his heart.

Can this kid really solve this problem that has been unsolved for eight thousand years? Can a substitute for the Grimoire really appear? If he really solves this problem, how can I praise him? The most important thing was that if this task was completed by him, according to the ancestral motto, I have to take him there?

Forget it, I’ll just let him go back quickly. It would be really bad should this kid manage to figure it out.

His Majesty coughed slightly, “It’s not early, so just go back first. You are mainly busy with the Dragon Knights Legion Army, so let this go for now!”

“Wait!” Yue Yang jumped up suddenly excitedly, “I have an idea, but I don’t know if it can be done!”

“Seriously? That fast?” His Majesty exclaimed. He immediately suppressed his emotions and returned to normal as he asked, “You thought of a way so quickly? What kind of idea is it?”

“I won’t tell you yet. Just wait until I make a sample, I hope it works! God, if this works…” Yue Yang ran out like a lunatic.

“This kid is a bit unbelievable,” His Majesty said so, but there was an inexplicable confidence for Yue Yang in his heart.

[TL note]:

At this point there’s more new characters that are emerging so I’m kind of making the glossary instead of referring to it. I’ll try my best to provide them as accurately as I can, so let me know if any of the names seem awkward or don’t flow all that well!

At any rate, enjoy the chapter! Hope you guys have a great day/night ahead of you!

P:S Really need help with the Hua Xu Ri guy, even Wikifandom is contradicting so, yea. Thanks!

-Sterling and Welp

chapters.title: Chapter 880 Get Out!
chapters.body:
Chapter 880 Get Out!

Chapter 880: Get Out!

T/L, “Thanks for the information guys!!! I got that Emperor thing cleared up, will be using the ‘he’ pronouns on him until the author decides otherwise. Once again, thank you very much!!!”

Emperor, “See? That wasn’t so hard, was it?”

Yue Yang, mumbling, “You’re the one who’s confusing the crap out of everyone.”

Emperor, “Pardon, what was that?”

Yue Yang, “Nothing, Your Majesty, I was just expressing how lucky I am to be graced by your presence. But alas, the night is no longer young, and I shall regretfully take my leave.”

Yue Yang, *Exits Tian Luo Palace*

Emperor, “..brat.”

T/L, *Awkward laugh* “Well… Let’s just get into the story.”

The night was as cool as water and the moon was crooked like a hook.

The peaceful Tian Luo Palace suddenly heard a huge explosion that shocked the guards on duty.

Was there another enemy coming? The two generals on duty rushed to the scene to investigate the blast quickly and found that a dark shadow had just disappeared. As they were about to capture the thief, the Guardian War God appeared levitating in the air, saying that it was not an enemy attack but the Third Young Master who was experimenting. Then, the Guardian War God ordered the two guards to retreat.

The Third Young Master was a great master of making puppets of beasts, second only to the Ghost Genius Yue Gong who was well known in the Soaring Dragon Continent.

But what kind of research was he doing this time?

The explosion was powerful, so it seemed like it was to be a very high-level practice even though they wouldn’t know whether it was a success or a failure. The two generals on duty did not dare to ask more as they quickly left according to the order. The matter of the superiors was not something they could inquire about. Besides, there were two guards on duty that were waiting outside for them to appease.

Outside the Tian Luo Emperor Hua Xu Ri’s bedroom, student Yue Yang rushed in with tattered clothes.

The female officer on duty was dumbfounded.

She didn’t know whether to stop him or not.

She stared at Yue Yang zooming past the gates and managed to see it close. Well, it’s too late to stop him anyways.

Immediately turning her head back, she pretended that she didn’t see Yue Yang entering the chambers.

“What are you doing? I am already turning in,” His Majesty was furious— this kid even dared to run to his bed. This is too much!

“Don’t go to sleep yet, get up and help me check this out. Although it didn’t succeed, I found the right idea and I think it is very promising, so come and take a look. You can even give me some advice about it!” Clumps of dark matter in his hands were presented to him like treasure. Accepting or rejecting it would both be awkward, so he snorted angrily and waved a tremor of pure white energy from his hands. He then put the items from Yue Yang’s hand into it before pushing him away tenderly, making the veil over his bed shroud over a layer of mist.

“Nonsense, who allowed you to enter my private chambers? If you do this once more, you will be severely punished and not be forgiven!” His Majesty first criticized Yue Yang for his rudeness.

“Well, I just forgot about it this time out of happiness?” Although Yue Yang explained, he had no regrets.

“Then if I am happy at the moment, can I beat you up? What sophistry!” His Majesty would definitely be internally injured one day if the boy keeps angering him. However, His Majesty still knew who Yue Yang was. Although this kid was a mess in life, he was still very serious and focused when doing big things. If he was happy, it would not be very strange for him to have this kind of behavior of rushing to his bedroom at night as this kid was obnoxious by nature!

“Then, may Your Majesty forgive me just for this time!” Student Yue Yang apologized again and his attitude seemed very sincere.

There was a big explosion just now and he didn’t have time to wash his face.

All he cared about was running over to find him.

The explosion turned his face into a hue of charcoal as he showed himself with a bit of embarrassment and focused persistence. His Majesty saw it and couldn’t help but give him the benefit of the doubt. Technically, he became like this because of me. If it weren’t for me, he might have been in the arms of some beauty instead of coming into my palace to be lectured.

His Majesty wanted to be angry with this kid, but he just couldn’t.

When he saw his nose twitching, looking like he smelled something unusual, His Majesty coughed immediately and diverted Yue Yang’s attention, “I will not pursue the matter of barging into the palace at night. However, this time is the only exception; there are no do-overs.”

“I thank Your Majesty.”

When Yue Yang saw that His Majesty was not angry anymore, he immediately introduced his research results, “It would be impossible even if I used the entire powers of the Tong Tian Tower and the Heaven Realm to make a similar existence like the Grimoire; that is something that even a God is unable to accomplish, after all. So, I was thinking, why do we want to build castles in the sky in the first place? We don’t have such high technology and we certainly can’t build castles in the sky, so we can build them on the ground…Of course, this is just an analogy. What I mean is to use our current technology and capabilities to make substitutes that we can achieve.”

His Majesty’s interest was piqued by his words. Indeed, if it was too difficult to accomplish, why not change your mind and set the goal to something lower?

Yue Yang moved a stool and sat in front of the bed.

He then started talking about his own experience endlessly.

While talking, he also signalled the female officer on duty outside to come in and pour him a cup of tea.

The female officer heard His Majesty beckoned for her to enter as she came in with some tea. When she saw Yue Yang sitting in front of His Majesty’s bed, her eyes widened as if she saw a ghost.

Yue Yang took a sip of tea and even expressed that he was very dissatisfied with her poor service, but the female officer had hurriedly left, so there was no way for him to complain.

Seeing this, His Majesty almost missed the epiphany.

Did he just assume that he lives here now?

This kid is really making himself at home!

However, His Majesty, who was kept in anticipation by Yue Yang’s interest at this time, did not chase him out. Instead, he listened while studying the semi-finished products in his hands.

Just like the inaugural speech of the president of the United States that he had seen before, Yue Yang talked with excitement and fluttered his fist, “It is nothing but a pipe dream for us to make a treasure or something that can contract multiple Battle-type Beasts! Even the ancient Grimoires created by the Gods only have a Life Guardian beast for each person. Hence, we should not think about how to surpass or approach a goal like that before we have that powerful ability. However, we can reflect on one point here, since the Grimoires and the Life Guardian Beasts exist, and the attributes and characteristics of each of them differs with every individual, what does it mean? It means that the potential and characteristics of each person are different, or at least on the aspect of the soul, the body would be different. On the other hand, the things you learn from the day after tomorrow remain the same, but the things you learn from the day after tomorrow are unable to change the truth.”

The more His Majesty listened, the more he liked it. There was a feeling of inspiration flashing in his mind as he felt that there needed to be something a little more.

He couldn’t help it, so he cooperated with Yue Yang’s speech and asked, “So what did you find out?”

Yue Yang shook his fist, “I dare not be 100% sure, but I am 90% sure. It can prove the fact that everyone’s soul has the potential of a beast contract. At the very least, most people would have this possibility,”

His Majesty could not help but exclaim at Yue Yang’s amazing remarks, “This is impossible, what are you using to prove it?”

Yue Yang smiled very confidently.

Although his face was dark, he was still full of charms. A confident light shimmered in his eyes that were as bright as a star!

He stood up, waved his fist heavily, and strengthened his tone, “First of all, let’s think about why some people can form a Grimoire Contract while some people can’t. Maybe many warriors will say that it depends on the bloodline heritage. That is one of the aspects in itself, but more people would consciously realize that it mainly depended on a person’s potential. This potential is linked to all aspects and blood is just one kind of it. In other words, people who can form the Grimoire Contracts must be relatively excellent in all aspects! Let us go beyond— looking at the problem in this way, if everyone’s soul can form a Grimoire Contract, but only very few people can realize their potential…”

His Majesty understood this immediately.

A kind of perplexity that has not been realized for a long time, as if the sun had shown itself from behind the mask of clouds.

It was like the opening of a window. His Majesty felt his eyes suddenly open up. Perhaps the things that he had been pursuing so hard to break through were finally starting to show signs of penetration.

“You mean, is it because the human body restricts the contact between the soul and the Grimoire? Because the human body isolates the communication between the soul and the Grimoire? Is it because of the lack of personal potential that most people can’t make a contract?” His Majesty’s voice became excited as he finally found a path to walk on. Once this mystery was broken, many things became simple.

“That’s right,” Yue Yang clapped his hands and affirmed, “One more question, why can only people under the age of 20 be able to contract the Grimoire? Of course, in the Heaven Realm or the other races, this restriction does not exist as even 100-year-olds can still contract with Grimoires.”

“This is because of the influence of the ‘day after tomorrow’!” His Majesty immediately thought of the most obvious answer.

“Correct,” Yue Yang praised, “For example, if in infancy, almost all people have the potential of the Grimoire Contract. Then, with the passage of time and the growth of the ‘day after tomorrow’, if a person’s potential is not tapped into, it will be consumed and buried, maybe even buried by acquired knowledge and ideas. Why do younger generations of large families have more opportunities to contract a Grimoire? One is because of the excellent bloodline, meaning the potential is sufficient; the second is because of proper training; the third is because of self-confidence— that’s right, it’s self-confidence! Ordinary people don’t have the self-confidence of contracting a Grimoire at all, and immortal belief is the first foundation of Innate Supremacy. Without sufficient self-confidence, you will definitely not be able to contract a Grimoire. Of course, just having self-confidence with no potential or obsession will become an acquired greed or some kind of desire. This would bscures the innate potential and severely isolate the communication of the soul, which is also the focus of the inability to contract a Grimoire. The final reason is that wisdom can be divided into two types— one is knowledge, and the other is intelligence. The lack of the former can be made up by learning, just like the evolution of Battle Beasts to Holy Beasts and Divine Beasts. But if intelligence was lacking, it would be inherently insufficient and become naturally impossible to communicate with souls.”

“Now I understand a little bit why the predecessors put the ranks on the ‘Innate’, ” His Majesty suddenly realized as he screamed out, “Enlightened martial artists are called Innate martial artists and the higher level is called Divine Supreme. It turned out to be us who have to work to get to the innate levels.”

“We have figured out the truth. In fact, many people can form a contract with the Grimoire, but due to the influence of the concept of the ‘day after tomorrow’ and the various influences in growth, the vast majority of people have been sadly reduced to mediocrity,” Yue Yang said excitedly and moved the stool a little bit forward, “If we identified the problem, we can start researching. My idea is to make something that can communicate with souls and can be used by ordinary warriors, just like what you mentioned. Strictly speaking, it won’t be a Grimoire but a summoning item, just like summoning magic crystals. However, the biggest advantage is that it communicates with the soul. The contracted beast would be similar to a Life Guardian Beast, and it is a beast that is closest to its owner’s body attributes and most in line with the growth of one’s body…”

“Your black metal ball is the substitute you were talking about?” His Majesty inspected it carefully but couldn’t see what was special about it.

“What do you mean ‘black metal ball’? It just hasn’t succeeded yet!” Yue Yang was stunned at first. However, he collected himself and explained, “Its function is like this— it is an instrument worn on the wrist. An ordinary warrior or the younger generations who are unable to contract the Grimoire can’t communicate with souls, so we don’t need them to do this. This wrist instrument has a special design to communicate with the soul. We are like seeds, putting our consciousness on the wrist mechanism, whereas it is equivalent to one of our puppet summoned beasts. But ultimately, its function would be to awaken the user’s summoned beast in the body.”

“You mean, this thing is a mechanism?” His Majesty was rather intelligent.

“That’s right,” Student Yue Yang’s stool moved forward slightly, “Comparing this thing to a crossbow, then the summoned beast would be an arrow. As long as you pull the trigger gently, you can launch the crossbow arrow. You don’t need to know how to make a crossbow, and you don’t need to know how to make an arrow either. You wouldn’t need to know anything except how to pull the trigger.”

“According to you, it might just be possible…The problem is, if that is the case, then you are going to be working very hard because everything is provided by you,” His Majesty was a little touched. If this was truly the case, then Yue Yang would be the biggest contributor as this was a purely labor-consuming and unfavorable work.

“I thought you were going to criticize this warrior, claiming that I will control the entire Soaring Dragon Continent in the future!” Yue Yang secretly wiped a cold sweat.

“Do you also have ambitions besides those full of lust?” His Majesty wanted to be serious, but he couldn’t help being giddy.

“Hey, I’m an upright gentleman,” Student Yue Yang’s biggest wish was to pretend to be Liu Xiahui [1] and have beautiful women sit in his arms.

T/L Note: [1] Someone from ancient Literature of China. His name is used to refer to someone as a gentleman, upright, rule-abiding etc etc.

“You? Forget it! But, do you really not need to be paid?” His Majesty suddenly felt a bit of a headache. If this pioneering work can change the entire Soaring Dragon Continent to become successful, what should I award this kid? He lacks nothing!

“Making a puppet Battle-type Beast is very simple, but it is really troublesome to detect the potential and plant the rune markings to communicate with the soul. I suggest that it only be made for beautiful women. Your Majesty, the beautiful women will then be divided amongst the both of us. If you are more capable, you can also have 60% and I’ll take the remaining 40%! Think about it— a large group of beauties surrounding you, the kind of feeling of them drowning you in a sea of milk…” Student Yue Yang couldn’t help but get up.

“What are you talking about?” Your Majesty was angry as he knew that this kid had no good intentions.

“Your Majesty, you hide in your harem every day, so don’t tell me that you don’t like beautiful women. Forget it, you and I are both men, so I understand you. Apologies, I only said that because you are a confidant. Had you been someone like Jun Wu You, I wouldn’t have bothered to care about him. By the way, you may belong to the type that is secretly and silently enthusiastic, so you should communicate with others more. Otherwise, if you keep this up for a long time, you will definitely become mentally unhealthy!” Yue Yang slapped his chest, implying that men should not beat around the bush— they should be daring enough to say it, to love, and if they liked beauties, they should loudly proclaim it as well.

“Get out!” His Majesty was furious. He wanted to rush out of the bed’s veiled tent to kick this kid out of the hall. He dared to say that I’m the type who was secretly enthusiastic? He’s practically asking for death!

[TL note]:

At this point there’s more new characters that are emerging so I’m kind of making the glossary instead of referring to it. I’ll try my best to provide them as accurately as I can, so let me know if any of the names seem awkward or don’t flow all that well!

Once again, thanks for clarifying! Hope you liked that little comical snippet I added above as well. Hope you guys have a great day/night ahead of you!

-Sterling

chapters.title: Chapter 881 Dragon'S Descent From The Heavens
chapters.body:
Chapter 881 Dragon'S Descent From The Heavens

Chapter 881: Dragon’s Descent From The Heavens

The Beast Wrist Instrument still had to go through continuous research. Although the direction of the answer was correct, there was still a long way to go before they could achieve success.

Before achieving that goal, even if Yue Yang was bold enough, he would no longer harass His Majesty anymore.

He guessed that His Majesty was really angry.

Otherwise, he wouldn’t rush over to kick him out.

Although he had the Heaven Eyes Transverse, student Yue Yang knew of the ‘feigned confusion’ technique. He pretended not to see a dark figure rushing out and even pretended not to know anything.

Yue Yang wanted to immerse himself in researching to get some results, but the old fox pulled him away. Now was an important time to publicize the Dragon Knights Legion Army, so how could they lose the righteous master, Yue Yang?

During the dusk of the following day.

“What is that?”

“It looks a bit like a dark cloud, but the movement is too weird.”

“It’s not a dark cloud at all, it’s a bird. Oh my goodness, no, it’s not a bird— it’s a flying dragon! The Flying Dragons are covering the sky!”

The mercenaries who were resting in the large square outside the Tong Tian Tower found a dark cloud rushing forward. When they got closer, they discovered that there were thousands of Flying Dragons. All kinds of Flying Dragons were soaring over the sky, making the mercenaries almost pee their pants in fright even though the dragons had no intentions of launching an attack. One flying dragon was already enough to destroy a mercenary group, so how many Flying Dragons were there? There were too many to calculate, but there are at least 10,000. If they were to attack, they could probably destroy a large city or even a country. The most terrifying thing was the fact that in this cluster of nearly 10,000 Flying Dragons, there were hundreds of Flying Dragons Kings that were as high-levelled as the Gold Ranks.

These Flying Dragon Kings were not only considered Gold Ranked Beast Kings, many of them possessed high intelligence and each of them had the strength that was equal to or higher than the Innate Realms of human warriors.

The front row of Flying Dragons had knights on their backs.

There were those who were old and there were those of the young; some men, and some were women.

Most of them were tall and strong female warriors.

These female warriors were easily recognized by some of the mercenary leaders as none other than the Heaven Realm Female Draconians that were under the command of the Third Young Master. This group of female warriors were powerful but extremely humble.

They were powerful, but they regarded themselves as slaves and never fought over anything.

However, they only recognized the Third Young Master of the Yue Clan. Besides him, no one else in the entire Tong Tian Tower could give them commands. The female warrior wearing a crown who was riding the Poisonous Flying Dragon King was none other than Queen Qi Ge.

Not far behind her, there stood a wise sage on the back of the Flying Plasma Dragon. The mercenaries also recognized him as the acting dean of the Ivy Academy, Shui Dong Liu, who soon became an important pillar of the Innate Alliance. The great sage was also one of the mentors recognized by the Third Young Master of the Yue Clan.

Although the sage Shui Dong Liu was not well-known before, he was extraordinary in the field of his strength and had overwhelming wisdom that was as broad as the sea.

The outstanding generation of Tong Tian Tower, like the Third Young Master of the Yue Clan, Xue Tan Lang, Yan Po Jun, Feng Qi Sha, Hai Da fu, Ye Kong, Li Qie, Li Jia and many more, were all his disciples.

“Roar!”

Just when the mercenaries looked up at the sky, thinking about which big shot decided to grace them with their presence, a dragon’s roar echoed from the sky.

A black smudge was descending quickly.

The strong wind blew as there was a sudden drop of deterrence that no one could resist. Not only did the mercenaries present feel it, but everyone felt a strong sense of suffocation.

The 30-meter-wide dragon wings unfolded, looking almost larger than the wingspan of three ordinary Flying Dragons. The golden scales resembled the sun, brightly dazzling to the point where they could barely open their eyes. A roar resounded like a bolt from a blue sky that even shocked the momentum brought by the 10,000 Flying Dragons, overshadowing them. What peered out from the clouds was a Giant Golden Dragon that was probably capable of engulfing the entire world as its strength was as high as the Platinum Rank Level 10. For thousands of years, no such imperial Battle Beast had ever appeared in the Soaring Dragon Continent. It was quite unexpected to have 10,000 Flying Dragons show up today, let alone a Giant Golden Dragon’s descent.

Moreover, it was still in the posture of being a mount.

In the entire Soaring Dragon Continent, who else could subdue such a beast into their own mount?

Of course it would be the Third Young Master of the Yue Clan. Besides him, who else had such strength? Apart from him, who else had such qualifications?

“Ahhhhh…”

“The Giant Dragon! It really is a Giant Golden Dragon!”

The mercenaries were excited and elated. They wanted to cheer but worried that the noise would seem disrespectful to the strong.

As soon as the Giant Golden Dragon with Innate Ranked abilities descended, the 10,000 other Flying Dragons immediately split from their formations and automatically arranged in groups. They drooped their heads and were revealing their humility, paying their tributes to the more advanced beings one after another.

The Heaven Realm Female Draconians that were riding behind the Flying Dragons shed tears when they watched Yue Yang emerge from his position of riding the dragon.

Descending the skies on a mighty dragon; this is the Sky Dragon Emperor that we have in our hearts!

They waved their shiny swords and shouted in unison.

Their frantic screaming was not only meant to pay tributes to the Lord, but it was also meant to celebrate the return of the legend that had been passed down from generations to generations!

The Sky Dragon Emperor is back!

He had already disappeared in the legend, but today, with a brand-new form, the Sky Dragon Emperor has descended from above and is back with them!

The cries of the Heaven Realm Female Draconians were intertwined with the tributing roars from the Flying Dragons. The sound could very well be described as earth-shattering as it reached the heavens and shook the entire ground. Below, whether it was a Flying Beast or the non-Flying Beasts, all of them had automatically descended to the ground with a fearful and respectful attitude in front of the Giant Golden Dragon controlled by the Third Young Master of the Yue Clan. They saluted and bowed to this powerful creature, expressing their humbleness to the extremely strong one who controlled it.

They were not qualified to salute this overbearing power because the emperor that they were paying their tributes to was only the mount of the strong one.

Ordinary merchants and servants were already frightened and paralyzed on the ground. Under the frightening aura of the dragon, even its inadvertent eyes looking down made them frightened. The mercenaries were a little better as they were accustomed to licking blood with the head of their knives. Although they were also scared in their hearts, their excitement triumphed over their fears. Countless mercenaries couldn’t help kneeling down, worshiping the young man who stood on the back of the Innate Giant Golden Dragon like a God with a kind of piety that they had never felt before.

What constitutes as the strong?

This was the true definition of the strong! The truly powerful warrior!

People looked up to the sky as the Giant Golden Dragon was shining brightly under the warm sunlight of the setting sun.

Those rays of light surrounded the man that was no longer unfamiliar to anyone. Maybe the omnipotent Gods in the legend were like this. No, it must have been like this!

“The Third Young Master of the Yue Clan is riding a Giant Golden Dragon of a high rank, just like an emperor, leading tens of thousands of Flying Dragons as they come out from the Tong Tian Tower to return to the Da Xia Empire!”

“The Da Xia Emperor Jun Wu You personally led the crowd to greet him.”

“The Four Major Families are gathering again! And nearly a hundred families and tycoons were there to welcome them!”

A series of explosive news blasted like a serial cannon and spreaded at the fastest of speeds. For a moment, almost all the taverns and all public places in all cities were discussing this shocking news. There was no false exaggeration and false rumors as it was a direct announcement made by the Three Major Guilds. The Warrior Guild, Mercenary Guild, and the Thief Guild had the exact same news: the Third Young Master of the Yue Clan was preparing to form a Dragon Knight Legion Army. There were no restrictions on gender, status, strength, age etc; everything was not restricted as it was meant to find and nurture promising elites.

In almost every tavern, there were propaganda signs posted for free by the Three Major Guilds at the door:

“Come join the Dragon Knight Legion Army, what are you waiting for?

The next elite is you!

You may not know how much potential you have, you may never know what realms you can achieve in the future— but all of that doesn’t matter. As long as you are willing to take a step, you will have the life you dream of. Even if you are no more than mediocre, as long as you have an unwavering will, the Third Young Master will provide everything you need!

You don’t need to pay anything at all. As long as you have a heart that will always grow stronger, then you are the next Dragon Knight or the next Dragon Blood Guard!

If you have no ambitions, why not let your children try it? Maybe the next glorious Dragon Knight is none other than your son or daughter!

Fathers who look forward to their sons becoming dragons [1] , the best method is right in front of you!

T/L Note: [1] Dragons are often associated with good luck there, so to say that they want their sons to be dragons, it means hoping they’ll succeed and prosper.

Your sons need this opportunity!”

…In almost all public places, the people could see propaganda slogans like this.

To say that one was not tempted would be a lie.

There has never been a person so generous as the Third Young Master of the Yue Clan. The test to determine their potential was already free, and yet he provided each warrior with a Flying Dragon and a glorious Dragon Knight identity. He did not charge them as well. The most important thing was that there had never been a person who gave ordinary people the opportunity to stand up like him.

Even if you can’t be a great Dragon Knight, your children could enter and become one of them!

If things really didn’t work out, being a servant of the Dragon Knight or a stableboy would be at least a hundred times better than waiting to die helplessly. Who would pity the civilians who were struggling for survival at the bottom of the food chain? Who would give the opportunity to those mercenaries whose blades licked endless amounts of blood? Who would care for those refugees who live in slums, waiting to die? Since birth, apart from the Third Young Master of the Yue clan, who else was willing to give any others the fate of turning over a new leaf again? Who possessed the ability to do this?

No one but the Third Young Master of the Yue Clan.

Only him… not only the unyielding spirit of martial arts, but also the will to survive, the belief in destiny and whatnot, had all exploded like a volcanic eruption, breaking out from the Soaring Dragon Continent.

Anyone walking on the street could see that the eyes of the lifeless passers-by flashed with a light that had never been seen before. It was a light of hope, a light that radiated from the bottom of their hearts.

In the Da Xia Palace’s main hall.

“You did this to put me on the spot— I’m protesting against it! I didn’t have much free time from the start, but thanks to you guys, I will be even more busy from now on,” Student Yue Yang was not as magnanimous as people think as he desperately resisted the idea of Jun Wu You and Old Fox, expressing his very unhappy demeanour. If it weren’t for Princess Qian Qian and Luo Hua, he would have beaten up these two old men.

“Except for some of the most important occasions, you don’t need to show up in ordinary times and you can take care of your affairs!” The Old Fox hurriedly comforted student Yue Yang who was about to run away.

“It is said that to become a god, it is very important to have the piety and integrity of many people,” Jun Wu You also helped.

“Damn, do you think that the enlightenment to the Divine Realm is like eating Chinese cabbages [2] ?” Yue Yang was speechless.

T/L Note: [2] In China, these Chinese cabbages are like staple dishes. They’re common, cheap and easy to manage if you’re a seller. Likewise, it’s referred to as being common or easy to achieve.

“Well, that’s just a matter of time!” Jun Wu You chuckled. As his father-in-law, he did not fight against Yue Yang at all.

“Little Yue Yang, it is very meaningful to do this,” As soon as Grandma Wu Teng spoke, Yue Yang couldn’t help but adhere to it. He sincerely respected this instructor, so he had to raise his hands in surrender and express his willingness to listen to everyone’s arrangements. In order to reward the well-behaved student Yue Yang, Princess Qian Qian lightly kicked him under the table. When Yue Yang went to see her, she turned her head and deliberately looked away to chat with Yue Bing.

However, the clever student Yue Yang still predicted what she had wanted to suggest.

It seems that Tigress will come to accompany me at night; what a profitable gain!

[TL note]:

At this point there’s more new characters that are emerging so I’m kind of making the glossary instead of referring to it. I’ll try my best to provide them as accurately as I can, so let me know if any of the names seem awkward or don’t flow all that well!

At any rate, enjoy the chapter! Hope you guys have a great day/night ahead of you!

-Sterling
